Conclusion: е≠¶дє†иЃЇзВєйЗНжЮДз°ЃеЃЮиГљеҐЮеЉЇLLMзЪДжЙєеИ§жАІжАЭзїіиГљеКЫпЉМGAARеЉХжУОеТМArguinasжХ∞жНЃйЫЖдЄЇж≠§жПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЙжХИеЈ•еЕЈпЉМдї£з†БеТМжХ∞жНЃйЫЖе∞ЖеЕђеЉАеПѓзФ®

Abstract: To think critically about arguments, human learners are trained to identify, reconstruct, and evaluate arguments. Argument reconstruction is especially important because it makes an argument's underlying inferences explicit. However, it remains unclear whether LLMs can similarly enhance their critical thinking ability by learning to reconstruct arguments. To address this question, we introduce a holistic framework with three contributions. We (1) propose an engine that automatically reconstructs arbitrary arguments (GAAR), (2) synthesize a new high-quality argument reconstruction dataset (Arguinas) using the GAAR engine, and (3) investigate whether learning argument reconstruction benefits downstream critical thinking tasks. Our experimental results show that, across seven critical thinking tasks, models trained to learn argument reconstruction outperform models that do not, with the largest performance gains observed when training on the proposed Arguinas dataset. The source code and dataset will be publicly available.

</details>


### [178] [TRiMS: Real-Time Tracking of Minimal Sufficient Length for Efficient Reasoning via RL](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17449)
*Tingcheng Bian,Jinchang Luo,Mingquan Cheng,Jinyu Zhang,Xiaoling Xia,Ni Li,Yan Tao,Haiwei Wang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇMSLзРЖиЃЇеЇ¶йЗПжЭ•йЗПеМЦжО®зРЖйУЊзЪДжЬАе∞ПеЕЕеИЖйХњеЇ¶пЉМеєґеЯЇдЇОж≠§еЉАеПСTRiMSжЦєж≥ХпЉМеЃЮзО∞80%дї•дЄКзЪДCoT tokenеЗПе∞СеРМжЧґдњЭжМБжИЦжПРеНЗеЗЖз°ЃзОЗгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ељУеЙНе§Іиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛйАЪињЗйХњжАЭзїійУЊеЃЮзО∞е§НжЭВжО®зРЖпЉМдљЖеѓЉиЗідЄ•йЗНзЪДжО®зРЖиЖ®иГАеТМиЃ°зЃЧеЖЧдљЩгАВйЬАи¶БжЬАе§ІеМЦ"жѓПtokenжЩЇиГљ"жЭ•еЗПе∞СдЄНењЕи¶БзЪДиЃ°зЃЧеЉАйФАгАВ

Method: 1. жПРеЗЇMSLзРЖиЃЇеЇ¶йЗПпЉМеЯЇдЇОзЛђзЂЛйЗЗж†ЈеЇПеИЧзЪДйАТељТеЃЪдєЙпЉМиѓБжШОеЕґжЮБйЩРе≠ШеЬ®пЉЫ2. еИЖжЮРдЄїжµБCoTеОЛзЉ©з≠ЦзХ•пЉМиѓЖеИЂжО•ињСMSLзЪДеЕ≥йФЃзїУжЮДеЫ†зі†пЉЫ3. жПРеЗЇTRiMSжЦєж≥ХпЉМзїУеРИGRPOзЃЧж≥ХеТМMSLдЉ∞иЃ°ињЫи°МиЃ≠зїГпЉМйАЪињЗеК®жАБжЙєжђ°иБЪеРИеТМеЯЇдЇОжЙєжђ°ж†ЗеЗЖеЈЃзЪДдЉШеКњиЃ°зЃЧжЭ•зЉУиІ£иЃ≠зїГдЄНз®≥еЃЪжАІгАВ

Result: еЬ®жЙАжЬЙеЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХдЄ≠еЃЮзО∞иґЕињЗ80%зЪДCoT tokenеЗПе∞СпЉМеРМжЧґеЗЖз°ЃзОЗжЬЙе∞ПеєЕжПРеНЗгАВ

Conclusion: MSLдЄЇжО®зРЖйУЊеОЛзЉ©жПРдЊЫдЇЖй¶ЦдЄ™еПѓжµЛйЗПзЪДдЄЛзХМпЉМTRiMSжЦєж≥ХиГљжЬЙжХИеЗПе∞СжО®зРЖиЖ®иГАпЉМеЬ®дњЭжМБеЗЖз°ЃжАІзЪДеРМжЧґжШЊиСЧйЩНдљОиЃ°зЃЧжИРжЬђгАВ

Abstract: Large language models achieve breakthroughs in complex reasoning via long chain-of-thought sequences. However, this often leads to severe reasoning inflation, causing substantial computational redundancy. To maximize Intelligence per Token, we introduce a theoretical metric, MSL-Minimal Sufficient Length. MSL rigorously characterizes the shortest reasoning length that preserves answer correctness. We provide a recursive definition based on independently sampled sequences and prove the existence of its limit, establishing the first measurable lower bound for reasoning-chain compression. Building on an analysis of mainstream CoT compression strategies, we identify key structural factors enabling a model to approach MSL. Based on these insights, we propose TRiMS which employs the GRPO algorithm in conjunction with MSL-based estimation during training, while mitigating instabilities during the training process through dynamic batch aggregation and advantage computation using batch-level standard deviation. TRiMS achieves over 80% CoT token reduction with a minor accuracy boost across all benchmarks.

</details>


### [179] [Humans and transformer LMs: Abstraction drives language learning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17475)
*Jasper Jian,Christopher D. Manning*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: GPT-2е∞Пж®°еЮЛе≠¶дє†иѓ≠и®Аз±їеИЂжЧґпЉМжКљи±°з±їеИЂи°МдЄЇжѓФеЕЈдљУиѓНж±Зи°МдЄЇеЗЇзО∞жЫіжЧ©пЉМдЄФдЄНеРМиѓ≠и®Аи°МдЄЇеЬ®иЃ≠зїГдЄ≠дЊЭжђ°з™БзДґжґМзО∞пЉМи°®жШОжКљи±°еМЦеЬ®иѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛе≠¶дє†дЄ≠иµЈеЕ≥йФЃдљЬзФ®гАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: з†Фз©ґиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛе¶ВдљХе≠¶дє†иѓ≠и®Аз±їеИЂпЉМйАЪињЗжѓФиЊГеЕґиЃ≠зїГињЗз®ЛдЄ≠зЪДи°МдЄЇдЄОдЇЇз±їиѓ≠и®Адє†еЊЧзЪДжКљи±°зЙєеЊБзРЖиЃЇеТМеЕЈдљУж†ЈдЊЛзРЖиЃЇпЉМжОҐзіҐиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛиГљеР¶дљЬдЄЇдЇЇз±їиѓ≠и®Адє†еЊЧж®°еЮЛзЪДе≠ШеЬ®иѓБжШОгАВ

Method: дљњзФ®еЯЇдЇОGPT-2е∞Пж®°еЮЛзЪДеЃЮй™МпЉМйАЪињЗжЦ∞йҐЦзЪДеЯЇдЇОеИЖж≠ІзЪДеЇ¶йЗПжЦєж≥ХињљиЄ™иЃ≠зїГињЗз®ЛдЄ≠зЪДе≠¶дє†иљ®ињєпЉМеИЖжЮРиѓНж±Зиѓ≠дєЙеТМеП•ж≥Хз±їеИЂзЪДжґМзО∞ж®°еЉПгАВ

Result: еПСзО∞дЄ§дЄ™еЕ≥йФЃзїУжЮЬпЉЪ1) ељУе≠¶дє†дЄАдЄ™зїУжЮДжЧґпЉМжКљи±°з±їеИЂе±ВйЭҐзЪДи°МдЄЇжѓФеЕЈдљУиѓНж±Зе±ВйЭҐзЪДи°МдЄЇеЗЇзО∞еЊЧжЫіжЧ©пЉЫ2) дЄНеРМзЪДиѓ≠и®Аи°МдЄЇеЬ®иЃ≠зїГзЪДдЄНеРМйШґжЃµдЊЭжђ°з™БзДґжґМзО∞гАВ

Conclusion: жКљи±°еМЦеЬ®иѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛе≠¶дє†иѓ≠и®Аз±їеИЂдЄ≠жЙЃжЉФеЕ≥йФЃиІТиЙ≤пЉМињЩдЄАеПСзО∞дЄЇиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдљЬдЄЇдЇЇз±їиѓ≠и®Адє†еЊЧж®°еЮЛзЪДе≠ШеЬ®иѓБжШОжПРдЊЫдЇЖйЗНи¶БдЊЭжНЃгАВ

Abstract: Categorization is a core component of human linguistic competence. We investigate how a transformer-based language model (LM) learns linguistic categories by comparing its behaviour over the course of training to behaviours which characterize abstract feature-based and concrete exemplar-based accounts of human language acquisition. We investigate how lexical semantic and syntactic categories emerge using novel divergence-based metrics that track learning trajectories using next-token distributions. In experiments with GPT-2 small, we find that (i) when a construction is learned, abstract class-level behaviour is evident at earlier steps than lexical item-specific behaviour, and (ii) that different linguistic behaviours emerge abruptly in sequence at different points in training, revealing that abstraction plays a key role in how LMs learn. This result informs the models of human language acquisition that LMs may serve as an existence proof for.

</details>


### [180] [Learning When to Attend: Conditional Memory Access for Long-Context LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17484)
*Sakshi Choudhary,Aditya Chattopadhyay,Luca Zancato,Elvis Nunez,Matthew Trager,Wei Xia,Stefano Soatto*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: L2AпЉИе≠¶дє†ж≥®жДПеКЫпЉЙжШѓдЄАзІНжЭ°дїґжАІйХњиМГеЫіиЃ∞ењЖиЃњйЧЃе±ВпЉМйАЪињЗе≠¶дє†еЖ≥еЃЪдљХжЧґи∞ГзФ®еЕ®е±Аж≥®жДПеКЫпЉМжШЊиСЧеЗПе∞СиЃ°зЃЧеЉАйФАпЉМе∞Жж®°еЮЛдЄКдЄЛжЦЗйХњеЇ¶дїО32KжЙ©е±ХеИ∞128KпЉМеРМжЧґдњЭжМБжАІиГљгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: иѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛйЪЊдї•ж≥ЫеМЦеИ∞йҐДиЃ≠зїГдЄКдЄЛжЦЗйХњеЇ¶дєЛе§ЦпЉМйЩРеИґдЇЖйХњиЈЭз¶їжО®зРЖеТМж£АзіҐиГљеКЫгАВдЉ†зїЯйХњдЄКдЄЛжЦЗиЃ≠зїГеЫ†ж≥®жДПеКЫжЬЇеИґзЪДдЇМжђ°жЦєе§НжЭВеЇ¶иАМдї£дїЈйЂШжШВпЉМдЄФе§Іе§ЪжХ∞tokenеЃЮйЩЕдЄКдЄНйЬАи¶БеЕ®е±Аж≥®жДПеКЫгАВ

Method: жПРеЗЇL2Aе±ВпЉМеЯЇдЇОиІВеѓЯеПСзО∞е§Іе§ЪжХ∞tokenеПѓдї•дЊЭиµЦе±АйГ®дЄКдЄЛжЦЗпЉМеЫ†ж≠§иЃЊиЃ°жЭ°дїґжАІпЉИtoken-wiseпЉЙйХњиМГеЫіиЃ∞ењЖиЃњйЧЃжЬЇеИґпЉМйАЪињЗе≠¶дє†еЖ≥еЃЪдљХжЧґи∞ГзФ®еЕ®е±Аж≥®жДПеКЫгАВињШеЉАеПСдЇЖеЃЪеИґзЪДTritonеЖЕж†ЄжЭ•йЂШжХИеЃЮзО∞ињЩзІНжЭ°дїґж≥®жДПеКЫгАВ

Result: еЬ®Qwen 2.5еТМQwen 3ж®°еЮЛдЄКпЉМе∞ЖжЬЙжХИдЄКдЄЛжЦЗйХњеЇ¶дїО32KжЙ©е±ХеИ∞128KпЉМжАІиГљдЄОж†ЗеЗЖйХњдЄКдЄЛжЦЗиЃ≠зїГзЫЄеЈЃеЬ®3%дї•еЖЕпЉМеРМжЧґдЄЇзЇ¶80%зЪДtokenиЈ≥ињЗеЕ®е±Аж≥®жДПеКЫгАВиЃ≠зїГеРЮеРРйЗПжѓФFlashAttentionжПРйЂШзЇ¶2еАНпЉМKVзЉУе≠ШеЖЕе≠ШеЗПе∞СиЊЊ50%гАВ

Conclusion: L2AйАЪињЗжЭ°дїґжАІеЕ®е±Аж≥®жДПеКЫжЬЇеИґпЉМеЬ®дњЭжМБжАІиГљзЪДеРМжЧґжШЊиСЧйЩНдљОдЇЖйХњдЄКдЄЛжЦЗиЃ≠зїГзЪДиЃ°зЃЧеЉАйФАеТМеЖЕе≠ШйЬАж±ВпЉМдЄЇйЂШжХИжЙ©е±Хиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдЄКдЄЛжЦЗйХњеЇ¶жПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЙжХИиІ£еЖ≥жЦєж°ИгАВ

Abstract: Language models struggle to generalize beyond pretraining context lengths, limiting long-horizon reasoning and retrieval. Continued pretraining on long-context data can help but is expensive due to the quadratic scaling of Attention. We observe that most tokens do not require (Global) Attention over the entire sequence and can rely on local context. Based on this, we propose L2A (Learning To Attend), a layer that enables conditional (token-wise) long-range memory access by deciding when to invoke global attention. We evaluate L2A on Qwen 2.5 and Qwen 3 models, extending their effective context length from 32K to 128K tokens. L2A matches the performance of standard long-context training to within 3% while skipping Global Attention for $\sim$80% of tokens, outperforming prior baselines. We also design custom Triton kernels to efficiently implement this token-wise conditional Attention on GPUs, achieving up to $\sim$2x improvements in training throughput and time-to-first-token over FlashAttention. Moreover, L2A enables post-training pruning of highly sparse Global Attention layers, reducing KV cache memory by up to 50% with negligible performance loss.

</details>


### [181] [Inducing Epistemological Humility in Large Language Models: A Targeted SFT Approach to Reducing Hallucination](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17504)
*Cem Uluoglakci,Tugba Taskaya Temizel*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: иѓ•иЃЇжЦЗжПРеЗЇдЇЖHypoTermInstructжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉМйАЪињЗиЃ≠зїГж®°еЮЛиѓЖеИЂиЗ™иЇЂзЯ•иѓЖиЊєзХМжЭ•еЗПе∞СеєїиІЙпЉМеЬ®е§ЪдЄ™ж®°еЮЛдЄКжШЊиСЧжПРеНЗеєїиІЙж£АжµЛеИЖжХ∞пЉМеРМжЧґдњЭжМБйАЪзФ®иГљеКЫз®≥еЃЪгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е§Іиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛзїПеЄЄдЇІзФЯеєїиІЙпЉМиЊУеЗЇжµБзХЕдљЖйФЩиѓѓзЪДдњ°жБѓпЉМйГ®еИЖеОЯеЫ†жШѓзЫСзЭ£еЊЃи∞ГпЉИSFTпЉЙйЪРеЉПе•ЦеК±ж®°еЮЛжАїжШѓеЫЮз≠ФгАВйЬАи¶БжХЩеѓЉж®°еЮЛиЃ§иѓЖиЗ™иЇЂзЯ•иѓЖиЊєзХМеєґжЙњиЃ§дЄНз°ЃеЃЪжАІгАВ

Method: еИЫеїЇHypoTermInstruct SFTжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉИ31,487дЄ™еЫЮз≠ФеѓєеЇФ11,151дЄ™йЧЃйҐШпЉЙпЉМеМЕеРЂеЕ≥дЇОдЄНе≠ШеЬ®зЪД"еБЗиЃЊ"жЬѓиѓ≠зЪДйЧЃйҐШпЉМжХЩеѓЉж®°еЮЛиЃ§иѓЖзЯ•иѓЖиЊєзХМгАВеРМжЧґеПСеЄГHypoTermQA-EnhancedеЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХгАВеЬ®Llama3.1-8BеТМGemma3-4Bж®°еЮЛдЄКињЫи°М800жђ°еПЧжОІLoRA SFTеЃЮй™МпЉМжµЛиѓХ100зІНеЊЃи∞ГйЕНзљЃгАВ

Result: зФ®HypoTermInstructжЫњжНҐйАЪзФ®жМЗдї§жХ∞жНЃжШЊиСЧжПРеНЗHypoTermеИЖжХ∞пЉИдЄ≠дљНжХ∞жПРеНЗ0.19%еИ∞25.91%пЉЙеТМFactScoreпЉИжПРеНЗ0.39%еИ∞0.86%пЉЙпЉМеРМжЧґMMLUжАІиГљдњЭжМБз®≥еЃЪпЉИдїЕдЄЛйЩН0.26%еИ∞0.35%пЉЙгАВ

Conclusion: йТИеѓєжАІзЪДйЂШиі®йЗПSFTжХ∞жНЃжХЩеѓЉеЕГиЃ§зЯ•жКАиГљеПѓдї•жЬЙжХИеЗПе∞СеєїиІЙпЉМжЧ†йЬАеБПе•љ/RLжµБз®ЛпЉМдЄЇжЮДеїЇжЫіеПѓйЭ†зЪДAIз≥їзїЯжПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЇеИґжАІиІБиІ£еТМеЃЮзФ®иЈѓеЊДгАВ

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) often hallucinate, producing fluent but false information, partly because supervised fine-tuning (SFT) implicitly rewards always responding. We introduce $\textit{HypoTermInstruct}$, an SFT dataset (31,487 responses for 11,151 questions) designed to teach models epistemological humility-the ability to recognize the limits of their own knowledge and admit uncertainty. This is achieved through questions about non-existent "hypothetical" terms. We also release $\textit{HypoTermQA-Enhanced}$, a benchmark for hallucination tendency strengthened through multiple validations. We conducted 800 controlled LoRA SFT runs across $\textit{Llama3.1-8B}$ and $\textit{Gemma3-4B}$ (base and instruct), testing 100 fine-tuning configurations with paired controls. Our results demonstrate that replacing generic instruction data with $\textit{HypoTermInstruct}$ significantly improves the HypoTerm Score (median increases of 0.19% to 25.91%) and FactScore (+0.39% to +0.86%), while maintaining stable performance on MMLU (minimal decreases of 0.26% to 0.35%). Our work demonstrates that targeted, high-quality SFT data teaching meta-cognitive skills can effectively reduce hallucination without preference/RL pipelines, providing mechanistic insights and a practical path toward more reliable AI systems.

</details>


### [182] [Language on Demand, Knowledge at Core: Composing LLMs with Encoder-Decoder Translation Models for Extensible Multilinguality](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17512)
*Mengyu Bu,Yang Feng*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: XBridgeпЉЪдЄАзІНзїДеРИеЉПзЉЦз†БеЩ®-LLM-иІ£з†БеЩ®жЮґжЮДпЉМеИ©зФ®йҐДиЃ≠зїГзњїиѓСж®°еЮЛе§ДзРЖе§Ъиѓ≠и®АдїїеК°пЉМдњЭжМБLLMдљЬдЄЇиЛ±иѓ≠ж†ЄењГе§ДзРЖйАЪзФ®зЯ•иѓЖпЉМйАЪињЗиљїйЗПзЇІжШ†е∞Де±ВиІ£еЖ≥и°®з§ЇеѓєйљРйЧЃйҐШ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е∞љзЃ°е§Іиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛпЉИLLMsпЉЙи°®зО∞еЗЇеЉЇе§ІзЪДйАЪзФ®жЩЇиГљпЉМдљЖеЕґе§Ъиѓ≠и®АжАІиГљйЂШеЇ¶дЄНеє≥и°°гАВиЩљзДґLLMsеЬ®зїЯдЄАиѓ≠дєЙз©ЇйЧідЄ≠зЉЦз†БдЇЖе§ІйЗПиЈ®иѓ≠и®АзЯ•иѓЖпЉМдљЖеЬ®е§ДзРЖдљОиµДжЇРжИЦжЬ™иІБиѓ≠и®АжЧґеЊАеЊАдЄНеПѓйЭ†гАВйҐДиЃ≠зїГзЪДзЉЦз†БеЩ®-иІ£з†БеЩ®зњїиѓСж®°еЮЛеЈ≤еЕЈе§Зеє≥и°°зЪДе§Ъиѓ≠и®АиГљеКЫпЉМињЩдЄЇи°•еЕЕLLMsжПРдЊЫдЇЖиЗ™зДґжЦєж°ИгАВ

Method: жПРеЗЇXBridgeжЮґжЮДпЉМе∞Же§Ъиѓ≠и®АзРЖиІ£еТМзФЯжИРеНЄиљљзїЩе§ЦйГ®йҐДиЃ≠зїГзњїиѓСж®°еЮЛпЉМеРМжЧґдњЭзХЩLLMдљЬдЄЇиЛ±иѓ≠ж†ЄењГе§ДзРЖйАЪзФ®зЯ•иѓЖгАВдЄЇиІ£еЖ≥ж®°еЮЛйЧізЪДи°®з§ЇеѓєйљРйЧЃйҐШпЉМеЉХеЕ•иљїйЗПзЇІиЈ®ж®°еЮЛжШ†е∞Де±ВеТМеЯЇдЇОжЬАдЉШдЉ†иЊУзЪДеѓєйљРзЫЃж†ЗпЉМеЃЮзО∞зїЖз≤ТеЇ¶иѓ≠дєЙдЄАиЗіжАІгАВ

Result: еЬ®еЫЫдЄ™LLMдЄКзЪДе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзРЖиІ£гАБжО®зРЖгАБжСШи¶БеТМзФЯжИРеЃЮй™Ми°®жШОпЉМXBridgeдЉШдЇОеЉЇеЯЇзЇњжЦєж≥ХпЉМзЙєеИЂжШѓеЬ®дљОиµДжЇРеТМжЬ™иІБиѓ≠и®АдЄКи°®зО∞з™БеЗЇпЉМдЄФжЧ†йЬАйЗНжЦ∞иЃ≠зїГLLMгАВ

Conclusion: XBridgeйАЪињЗзїДеРИйҐДиЃ≠зїГзњїиѓСж®°еЮЛеТМLLMпЉМжЬЙжХИиІ£еЖ≥дЇЖLLMsе§Ъиѓ≠и®АжАІиГљдЄНеє≥и°°йЧЃйҐШпЉМдЄЇдљОиµДжЇРеТМжЬ™иІБиѓ≠и®АжПРдЊЫдЇЖеПѓйЭ†зЪДе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзФЯжИРиГљеКЫпЉМеРМжЧґдњЭжМБдЇЖLLMзЪДж†ЄењГзЯ•иѓЖе§ДзРЖдЉШеКњгАВ

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit strong general intelligence, yet their multilingual performance remains highly imbalanced. Although LLMs encode substantial cross-lingual knowledge in a unified semantic space, they often struggle to reliably interface this knowledge with low-resource or unseen languages. Fortunately, pretrained encoder-decoder translation models already possess balanced multilingual capability, suggesting a natural complement to LLMs. In this work, we propose XBridge, a compositional encoder-LLM-decoder architecture that offloads multilingual understanding and generation to external pretrained translation models, while preserving the LLM as an English-centric core for general knowledge processing. To address the resulting representation misalignment across models, we introduce lightweight cross-model mapping layers and an optimal transport-based alignment objective, enabling fine-grained semantic consistency for multilingual generation. Experiments on four LLMs across multilingual understanding, reasoning, summarization, and generation indicate that XBridge outperforms strong baselines, especially on low-resource and previously unseen languages, without retraining the LLM.

</details>


### [183] [Detecting the Machine: A Comprehensive Benchmark of AI-Generated Text Detectors Across Architectures, Domains, and Adversarial Conditions](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17522)
*Madhav S. Baidya,S. S. Baidya,Chirag Chawla*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жЬђжЦЗжПРеЗЇдЇЖдЄАдЄ™еЕ®йЭҐзЪДжЬЇеЩ®зФЯжИРжЦЗжЬђж£АжµЛеЯЇеЗЖпЉМиѓДдЉ∞дЇЖе§ЪзІНж£АжµЛжЦєж≥ХеЬ®иЈ®йҐЖеЯЯгАБиЈ®LLMеТМеѓєжКЧжАІеЬЇжЩѓдЄЛзЪДжАІиГљпЉМеПСзО∞зО∞жЬЙжЦєж≥ХеЬ®йҐЖеЯЯиљђзІїеТМиЈ®ж®°еЮЛж≥ЫеМЦжЦєйЭҐе≠ШеЬ®жШЊиСЧе±АйЩРжАІгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: йЪПзЭАе§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛзЪДењЂйАЯжЩЃеПКпЉМињЂеИЗйЬАи¶Бй≤Бж£ТдЄФеПѓж≥ЫеМЦзЪДжЬЇеЩ®зФЯжИРжЦЗжЬђж£АжµЛеЩ®гАВзО∞жЬЙеЯЇеЗЖйАЪеЄЄеЬ®зРЖжГ≥жЭ°дїґдЄЛиѓДдЉ∞еНХдЄ™ж£АжµЛеЩ®еЬ®еНХдЄ™жХ∞жНЃйЫЖдЄКзЪДжАІиГљпЉМжЬ™иГљиІ£еЖ≥иЈ®йҐЖеЯЯињБзІїгАБиЈ®LLMж≥ЫеМЦеТМеѓєжКЧжАІй≤Бж£ТжАІз≠ЙеЕ≥йФЃйЧЃйҐШгАВ

Method: жЮДеїЇдЇЖдЄАдЄ™зїЉеРИеЯЇеЗЖпЉМеЬ®дЄ§дЄ™иѓ≠жЦЩеЇУпЉИHC3еМЕеРЂ23,363дЄ™дЇЇз±ї-ChatGPTеѓєпЉМELI5еМЕеРЂ15,000дЄ™дЇЇз±ї-Mistral-7BеѓєпЉЙдЄКиѓДдЉ∞е§ЪзІНж£АжµЛжЦєж≥ХпЉЪзїПеЕЄеИЖз±їеЩ®гАБеЊЃи∞ГзЪДTransformerзЉЦз†БеЩ®пЉИBERTгАБRoBERTaгАБELECTRAгАБDistilBERTгАБDeBERTa-v3пЉЙгАБCNNгАБXGBoostй£Ож†ЉиЃ°йЗПж®°еЮЛгАБеЯЇдЇОеЫ∞жГСеЇ¶зЪДж£АжµЛеЩ®дї•еПКLLMдљЬдЄЇж£АжµЛеЩ®зЪДжПРз§ЇжЦєж≥ХгАВ

Result: Transformerж®°еЮЛеЬ®еИЖеЄГеЖЕжАІиГљжО•ињСеЃМзЊОпЉМдљЖеЬ®йҐЖеЯЯиљђзІїдЄЛжАІиГљдЄЛйЩНгАВXGBoostй£Ож†ЉиЃ°йЗПж®°еЮЛжАІиГљзЫЄељУдЄФдњЭжМБеПѓиІ£йЗКжАІгАВLLMеЯЇж£АжµЛеЩ®и°®зО∞дЄНдљ≥дЄФеПЧзФЯжИРеЩ®-ж£АжµЛеЩ®иЇЂдїљеБПеЈЃељ±еУНгАВеЯЇдЇОеЫ∞жГСеЇ¶зЪДжЦєж≥ХеЗЇзО∞жЮБжАІеПНиљђпЉИзО∞дї£LLMиЊУеЗЇжѓФдЇЇз±їжЦЗжЬђеЫ∞жГСеЇ¶жЫідљОпЉЙпЉМдљЖж†°ж≠£еРОдїНжЬЙжХИгАВж≤°жЬЙжЦєж≥ХиГљеЬ®иЈ®йҐЖеЯЯеТМиЈ®LLMжЇРдЄКеЃЮзО∞й≤Бж£Тж≥ЫеМЦгАВ

Conclusion: ељУеЙНжЬЇеЩ®зФЯжИРжЦЗжЬђж£АжµЛжЦєж≥ХеЬ®иЈ®йҐЖеЯЯеТМиЈ®ж®°еЮЛж≥ЫеМЦжЦєйЭҐе≠ШеЬ®жШЊиСЧе±АйЩРжАІпЉМйЬАи¶БеЉАеПСжЫій≤Бж£ТзЪДж£АжµЛжЦєж≥ХжЭ•иІ£еЖ≥ињЩдЇЫжМСжИШгАВXGBoostй£Ож†ЉиЃ°йЗПж®°еЮЛеЬ®жАІиГљеТМеПѓиІ£йЗКжАІдєЛйЧіжПРдЊЫдЇЖиЙѓе•љеє≥и°°пЉМиАМеЯЇдЇОеЫ∞жГСеЇ¶зЪДжЦєж≥ХйЬАи¶БйТИеѓєзО∞дї£LLMињЫи°Ми∞ГжХігАВ

Abstract: The rapid proliferation of large language models (LLMs) has created an urgent need for robust and generalizable detectors of machine-generated text. Existing benchmarks typically evaluate a single detector on a single dataset under ideal conditions, leaving open questions about cross-domain transfer, cross-LLM generalization, and adversarial robustness.
  We present a comprehensive benchmark evaluating diverse detection approaches across two corpora: HC3 (23,363 human-ChatGPT pairs) and ELI5 (15,000 human-Mistral-7B pairs). Methods include classical classifiers, fine-tuned transformer encoders (BERT, RoBERTa, ELECTRA, DistilBERT, DeBERTa-v3), a CNN, an XGBoost stylometric model, perplexity-based detectors, and LLM-as-detector prompting.
  Results show that transformer models achieve near-perfect in-distribution performance but degrade under domain shift. The XGBoost stylometric model matches performance while remaining interpretable. LLM-based detectors underperform and are affected by generator-detector identity bias. Perplexity-based methods exhibit polarity inversion, with modern LLM outputs showing lower perplexity than human text, but remain effective when corrected. No method generalizes robustly across domains and LLM sources.

</details>


### [184] [AURORA Model of Formant-to-Tongue Inversion for Didactic and Clinical Applications](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17543)
*Patrycja Strycharczuk,Sam Kirkham*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: AURORAж®°еЮЛеЯЇдЇОеЙНдЄ§дЄ™еЕ±жМѓе≥∞йҐДжµЛеЕГйЯ≥еПСйЯ≥жЧґзЪДиИМдљНзІїеТМељҐзКґпЉМжЧ®еЬ®дљЬдЄЇжХЩе≠¶еЈ•еЕЈеТМзФЯзЙ©еПНй¶ИеЇФзФ®еЯЇз°АгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: еЉАеПСдЄАдЄ™иГље§ЯиІ£йЗКеЕ±жМѓе≥∞дЄОеПСйЯ≥дєЛйЧіеЕ≥з≥їзЪДжХЩе≠¶еЈ•еЕЈпЉМеєґдЄЇиѓ≠йЯ≥зФЯзЙ©еПНй¶ИеЇФзФ®жПРдЊЫеЯЇз°АпЉМеЄЃеК©иѓ≠йЯ≥е≠¶е≠¶зФЯгАБиѓ≠и®Ае≠¶еЃґдї•еПКи®Аиѓ≠ж≤їзЦЧдїОдЄЪиАЕеТМжВ£иАЕгАВ

Method: еЯЇдЇО40еРНиЛ±иѓ≠жѓНиѓ≠иАЕзЪДиґЕе£∞иИМжИРеГПеТМе£∞е≠¶жХ∞жНЃеїЇзЂЛж®°еЮЛпЉМйЗЗзФ®ж¶ВењµеТМиЃ°зЃЧеЯЇз°АзЫЄзїУеРИзЪДжЦєж≥ХпЉМеЉАеПСдЇЖShinyеЇФзФ®еТМеЃЮжЧґиИМзФЯзЙ©еПНй¶ИеОЯеЮЛиљѓдїґгАВ

Result: еїЇзЂЛдЇЖиГље§Яж†єжНЃеЙНдЄ§дЄ™еЕ±жМѓе≥∞еАЉйҐДжµЛиИМдљНзІїеТМељҐзКґзЪДж®°еЮЛпЉМеєґеЉАеПСдЇЖдљњж®°еЮЛжЫіжШУдЇОиЃњйЧЃзЪДеЈ•еЕЈпЉМињЫи°МдЇЖйТИеѓєйАЙеЃЪиИМзЙєеЊБзЪДеЃЪжАІиѓДдЉ∞гАВ

Conclusion: AURORAж®°еЮЛжИРеКЯеїЇзЂЛдЇЖеЕ±жМѓе≥∞дЄОиИМйГ®еПСйЯ≥дєЛйЧізЪДеЕ≥з≥їпЉМдЄЇиѓ≠йЯ≥е≠¶жХЩе≠¶еТМи®Аиѓ≠ж≤їзЦЧеЇФзФ®жПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЙдїЈеАЉзЪДеЈ•еЕЈпЉМйАЪињЗеЉАеПСзЪДеПѓиЃњйЧЃеЈ•еЕЈжЙ©е§ІдЇЖжљЬеЬ®зФ®жИЈзЊ§дљУгАВ

Abstract: This paper outlines the conceptual and computational foundations of the AURORA (Acoustic Understanding and Real-time Observation of Resonant Articulations) model. AURORA predicts tongue displacement and shape in vowel sounds based on the first two formant values. It is intended as a didactic aid helping to explain the relationship between formants and the underlying articulation, as well as a foundation for biofeedback applications. The model is informed by ultrasound tongue imaging and acoustic data from 40 native speakers of English. In this paper we discuss the motivation for the model, the modelling objectives as well as the model architecture. We provide a qualitative evaluation of the model, focusing on selected tongue features. We then present two tools developed to make the model more accessible to a wider audience, a Shiny app and a prototype software for real-time tongue biofeedback. Potential users include students of phonetics, linguists in fields adjacent to phonetics, as well as speech and language therapy practitioners and clients.

</details>


### [185] [Zipper-LoRA: Dynamic Parameter Decoupling for Speech-LLM based Multilingual Speech Recognition](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17558)
*Yuxiang Mei,Delai Qiu,Shengping Liu,Jiaen Liang,Yanhua Long*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: Zipper-LoRAпЉЪдЄАзІНзФ®дЇОе§Ъиѓ≠и®Аиѓ≠йЯ≥иѓЖеИЂзЪДеК®жАБзІ©зЇІиІ£иА¶ж°ЖжЮґпЉМйАЪињЗеЕ±дЇЂеТМиѓ≠и®АзЙєеЃЪе≠Рз©ЇйЧізЪДзїДеРИжЭ•иІ£еЖ≥жХ∞жНЃдЄНеє≥и°°дЄЛзЪДз®≥еЃЪжАІ-еПѓе°СжАІеЫ∞еҐГ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е§Ъиѓ≠и®Аиѓ≠йЯ≥е§Іиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛеЬ®жХ∞жНЃеИЖеЄГдЄНеє≥и°°жЧґйЭҐдЄіз®≥еЃЪжАІ-еПѓе°СжАІеЫ∞еҐГпЉЪеЃМеЕ®еЕ±дЇЂеПВжХ∞дЉЪеѓЉиЗідљОиµДжЇРиѓ≠и®АеПЧеИ∞иіЯеРСиЈ®иѓ≠и®Аеє≤жЙ∞пЉМиАМеЃМеЕ®иѓ≠и®АзЙєеЃЪи∞ГдЉШеПИйЩРеИґдЇЖжЬЙзЫКзЪДзЯ•иѓЖињБзІї

Method: жПРеЗЇZipper-LoRAж°ЖжЮґпЉМеМЕеРЂдЄЙзІНеПШдљУпЉИStaticгАБHardгАБSoftпЉЙпЉМйАЪињЗиљїйЗПзЇІиѓ≠и®АжЭ°дїґиЈѓзФ±еЩ®еЬ®LoRAзІ©зЇІеИЂеК®жАБеРИжИРеЕ±дЇЂеТМиѓ≠и®АзЙєеЃЪе≠Рз©ЇйЧізЪДжЫіжЦ∞пЉМеЃЮзО∞зїЖз≤ТеЇ¶еЕ±дЇЂдЄОиІ£иА¶гАВйЗЗзФ®дЄ§йШґжЃµиЃ≠зїГз≠ЦзХ•пЉИInitial-BйҐДзГ≠пЉЙеК†йАЯжФґжХЫ

Result: еЬ®12зІНиѓ≠и®АзЪДжЈЈеРИиµДжЇРиЃЊзљЃдЄ≠пЉМZipper-LoRAеЬ®еЃМеЕ®еЕ±дЇЂеТМзЛђзЂЛеЯЇзЇњжЦєж≥ХдЄКи°®зО∞жЫідЉШпЉМе∞§еЕґеЬ®жЮБдљОиµДжЇРеЬЇжЩѓдЄЛгАВиѓ•ж°ЖжЮґеЬ®еИЖеЭЧеТМйЭЮеИЖеЭЧзЉЦз†БеЩ®йЕНзљЃдЄ≠еЭЗдњЭжМБй≤Бж£ТжАІ

Conclusion: Zipper-LoRAйАЪињЗеК®жАБзІ©зЇІиІ£иА¶жЬЙжХИиІ£еЖ≥дЇЖе§Ъиѓ≠и®АASRдЄ≠зЪДжХ∞жНЃдЄНеє≥и°°йЧЃйҐШпЉМдЄЇе§ІиІДж®°еЃЮзФ®е§Ъиѓ≠и®Аиѓ≠йЯ≥иѓЖеИЂжПРдЊЫдЇЖеПѓйЭ†ж°ЖжЮґ

Abstract: Speech Large Language Models (Speech-LLMs) have emerged as a powerful approach for automatic speech recognition (ASR) by aligning speech encoders with large language models. However, adapting these systems to multilingual settings with imbalanced data distributions remains challenging. In such scenarios, a stability-plasticity dilemma often arises: fully shared Parameter-Efficient Fine-Tuning (PEFT) can cause negative inter-lingual interference for under-represented languages, while fully language-specific tuning limits the cross-lingual beneficial knowledge transfer needed for low-resource tasks. To address this, we propose Zipper-LoRA, a novel rank-level decoupling framework with three variants (Static, Hard, and Soft) that dynamically synthesizes LoRA updates from shared and language-specific subspaces. By using a lightweight language-conditioned router, Zipper-LoRA dynamically controls the contribution of each subspace at the LoRA rank level, enabling fine-grained sharing where languages are compatible and strict decoupling when conflicts occur. To further stabilize optimization under imbalanced data, we propose a two-stage training strategy with an Initial-B warm start that significantly accelerates convergence. Experiments on a 12-language mixed-resource setting show that Zipper-LoRA consistently outperforms both fully shared and independent baselines, particularly in extremely low-resource scenarios. Moreover, we demonstrate that these gains are robust across both chunked and non-chunked encoder configurations, confirming the framework's reliability for practical, large-scale multilingual ASR. Our code and data will be available at https://github.com/YuCeong-May/Zipper-LoRA for reproducibility.

</details>


### [186] [KA2L: A Knowledge-Aware Active Learning Framework for LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17566)
*Haoxuan Yin,Bojian Liu,Chen Tang,Yangfan Wang,Lian Yan,Jingchi Jiang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: KA2Lж°ЖжЮґйАЪињЗжљЬеЬ®з©ЇйЧіеИЖжЮРиѓДдЉ∞LLMеѓєзЙєеЃЪзЯ•иѓЖзВєзЪДжОМжП°з®ЛеЇ¶пЉМжЮДеїЇж®°еЮЛжЬ™жОМжП°зЪДйЧЃйҐШињЫи°МдЄїеК®е≠¶дє†пЉМеЗПе∞С50%ж†Зж≥®еТМиЃ°зЃЧжИРжЬђеєґжПРеНЗжАІиГљгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ељУеЙНз†Фз©ґзЉЇдєПеѓєLLMйҐЖеЯЯзЙєеЃЪзЯ•иѓЖзРЖиІ£жЈ±еЇ¶зЪДжОҐзіҐпЉМдї•еПКе¶ВдљХеЇФзФ®йТИеѓєжАІдЄїеК®е≠¶дє†жЭ•жПРеНЗеЕґдЄУдЄЪиГљеКЫгАВйЬАи¶БиІ£еЖ≥LLMзЯ•иѓЖжОМжП°иѓДдЉ∞еТМйЂШжХИиЃ≠зїГзЪДйЧЃйҐШгАВ

Method: жПРеЗЇзЯ•иѓЖжДЯзЯ•дЄїеК®е≠¶дє†(KA2L)ж°ЖжЮґпЉЪ1) дљњзФ®зЯ•иѓЖеИЖеЄГжОҐжµЛжКАжЬѓеИЖжЮРTransformerе±ВзЪДйЪРиЧПзКґжАБпЉМиѓЖеИЂеЈ≤зЯ•еТМжЬ™зЯ•зЯ•иѓЖеИЖеЄГпЉЫ2) жПРеЗЇйЪРиЧПзКґжАБиІ£з†БжЦєж≥ХпЉМдїОжљЬеЬ®зЯ•иѓЖз©ЇйЧізФЯжИРе§ІйЗПжЬ™зЯ•йЧЃйҐШзЪДиЗ™зДґиѓ≠и®Аи°®ињ∞гАВ

Result: еЬ®9дЄ™еЉАжЇРLLMдЄКзЪДеЃЮй™Ми°®жШОпЉМKA2LеЬ®дЄ§дЄ™еЉАжФЊеЯЯеТМдЄАдЄ™еЮВзЫіеЯЯжХ∞жНЃйЫЖдЄКжШЊиСЧеЗПе∞С50%зЪДж†Зж≥®еТМиЃ°зЃЧжИРжЬђпЉМеРМжЧґиОЈеЊЧжЫіе•љзЪДжАІиГљи°®зО∞гАВ

Conclusion: KA2Lж°ЖжЮґйАЪињЗеИЖжЮРLLMзЪДзЯ•иѓЖжОМжП°жГЕеЖµпЉМйТИеѓєжАІеЬ∞жЮДеїЇжЬ™жОМжП°йЧЃйҐШињЫи°МдЄїеК®е≠¶дє†пЉМжЬЙжХИжПРеНЗиЃ≠зїГжХИзОЗеТМж®°еЮЛжАІиГљпЉМдЄЇLLMзЪДдЄїеК®е≠¶дє†з≠ЦзХ•жПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЙдїЈеАЉзЪДиІБиІ£гАВ

Abstract: Fine-tuning large language models (LLMs) with high-quality knowledge has been shown to enhance their performance effectively. However, there is a paucity of research on the depth of domain-specific knowledge comprehension by LLMs and the application of targeted active learning to improve their expertise. To address this gap, we introduce the Knowledge-Aware Active Learning (KA2L) framework. This framework assesses LLMs' mastery of specific knowledge points to aid in constructing unanswerable or unknowable questions through latent space analysis. This active learning strategy enhances training efficiency by focusing on knowledge the model has yet to master, thereby minimizing redundancy in learning already acquired information. This study innovatively employs a knowledge distribution probing technique to examine the hidden states of specific Transformer layers and identify the distribution of known and unknown knowledge within the LLM. Additionally, a hidden-state decoding method is proposed to generate numerous unknown questions in natural language from the latent knowledge space. In our experiments, we selected nine open-source LLMs to validate the effectiveness of the proposed framework. Results indicate that KA2L not only significantly reduces 50% annotation and computation costs across two open-domain and one vertical-domain dataset but also achieves better performance, offering valuable insights into active learning strategies for LLMs. The code is available at https://anonymous.4open.science/r/KA2L-F15C.

</details>


### [187] [VeriAgent: A Tool-Integrated Multi-Agent System with Evolving Memory for PPA-Aware RTL Code Generation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17613)
*Yaoxiang Wang,Qi Shi,ShangZhan Li,Qingguo Hu,Xinyu Yin,Bo Guo,Xu Han,Maosong Sun,Jinsong Su*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇPPAжДЯзЯ•зЪДе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУж°ЖжЮґпЉМе∞ЖEDAеЈ•еЕЈйЫЖжИРеИ∞йЧ≠зОѓеЈ•дљЬжµБдЄ≠пЉМйАЪињЗињЫеМЦиЃ∞ењЖжЬЇеИґеЃЮзО∞жМБзї≠дЉШеМЦпЉМеЬ®дњЭжМБеКЯиГљж≠£з°ЃжАІзЪДеРМжЧґжШЊиСЧжПРеНЗPPAжМЗж†ЗгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: зО∞жЬЙLLMжЦєж≥ХдЄїи¶БеЕ≥ж≥®RTLдї£з†БзФЯжИРзЪДеКЯиГљж≠£з°ЃжАІпЉМдљЖењљиІЖдЇЖеЕ≥йФЃзЪДзЙ©зРЖиЃЊиЃ°зЫЃж†ЗпЉИеКЯиАЧгАБжАІиГљгАБйЭҐзІѓпЉЙгАВйЬАи¶Бе∞ЖPPAжМЗж†ЗзЇ≥еЕ•дЉШеМЦињЗз®ЛпЉМеЃЮзО∞жЫійЂШиі®йЗПзЪДVerilogдї£з†БзФЯжИРгАВ

Method: жПРеЗЇPPAжДЯзЯ•зЪДе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУж°ЖжЮґпЉМеМЕеРЂз®ЛеЇПеСШдї£зРЖгАБж≠£з°ЃжАІдї£зРЖеТМPPAдї£зРЖзЪДйЧ≠зОѓеЈ•дљЬжµБгАВеЉХеЕ•ињЫеМЦиЃ∞ењЖжЬЇеИґпЉМе∞ЖдЉШеМЦзїПй™Ме§ЦйГ®еМЦдЄЇзїУжЮДеМЦиЃ∞ењЖиКВзВєпЉМйАЪињЗдЄУзФ®еЖЕе≠ШзЃ°зРЖеЩ®еК®жАБзїіжК§иЃ∞ењЖж±†пЉМеЯЇдЇОеОЖеП≤жЙІи°Миљ®ињєдЉШеМЦз≠ЦзХ•гАВ

Result: еЃЮй™Ми°®жШОпЉМиѓ•жЦєж≥ХеЬ®дњЭжМБеЉЇе§ІеКЯиГљж≠£з°ЃжАІзЪДеРМжЧґпЉМеЬ®PPAжМЗж†ЗдЄКеПЦеЊЧдЇЖжШЊиСЧжФєињЫгАВйАЪињЗеЈ•еЕЈй©±еК®еПНй¶ИдЄОзїУжЮДеМЦеПѓињЫеМЦиЃ∞ењЖзЪДзїУеРИпЉМе∞ЖRTLзФЯжИРдїОдЄАжђ°жАІжО®зРЖиљђеПШдЄЇжМБзї≠еПНй¶Ий©±еК®зЪДдЉШеМЦињЗз®ЛгАВ

Conclusion: иѓ•ж°ЖжЮґдЄЇеЬ®еЃЮйЩЕз°ђдїґиЃЊиЃ°жµБз®ЛдЄ≠йГ®зљ≤LLMжПРдЊЫдЇЖеПѓжЙ©е±ХзЪДйАФеЊДпЉМйАЪињЗйЫЖжИРEDAеЈ•еЕЈеПНй¶ИеТМињЫеМЦиЃ∞ењЖжЬЇеИґпЉМеЃЮзО∞дЇЖеКЯиГљж≠£з°ЃжАІдЄОзЙ©зРЖжМЗж†ЗзЪДиБФеРИдЉШеМЦгАВ

Abstract: LLMs have recently demonstrated strong capabilities in automatic RTL code generation, achieving high syntactic and functional correctness. However, most methods focus on functional correctness while overlooking critical physical design objectives, including Power, Performance, and Area. In this work, we propose a PPA-aware, tool-integrated multi-agent framework for high-quality verilog code generation. Our framework explicitly incorporates EDA tools into a closed-loop workflow composed of a \textit{Programmer Agent}, a \textit{Correctness Agent}, and a \textit{PPA Agent}, enabling joint optimization of functional correctness and physical metrics. To support continuous improvement without model retraining, we introduce an \textit{Evolved Memory Mechanism} that externalizes optimization experience into structured memory nodes. A dedicated memory manager dynamically maintains the memory pool and allows the system to refine strategies based on historical execution trajectories. Extensive experiments demonstrate that our approach achieves strong functional correctness while delivering significant improvements in PPA metrics. By integrating tool-driven feedback with structured and evolvable memory, our framework transforms RTL generation from one-shot reasoning into a continual, feedback-driven optimization process, providing a scalable pathway for deploying LLMs in real-world hardware design flows.

</details>


### [188] [Do Language Models Encode Semantic Relations? Probing and Sparse Feature Analysis](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17624)
*Andor Diera,Ansgar Scherp*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: з†Фз©ґйАЪињЗзЇњжАІжОҐжµЛеТМжЬЇеИґеПѓиІ£йЗКжАІжКАжЬѓеИЖжЮРLLMе¶ВдљХзЉЦз†Биѓ≠дєЙеЕ≥з≥їпЉМеПСзО∞е±ВзЇІеЕ≥з≥їе≠ШеЬ®жЦєеРСдЄНеѓєзІ∞жАІпЉМеЕ≥з≥їдњ°еПЈеЬ®дЄ≠йЧіе±ВжЬАеЉЇпЉМдЄФдЄНеРМж®°еЮЛеЬ®еЫ†жЮЬжАІжЦєйЭҐе≠ШеЬ®иГљеКЫеЈЃеЉВгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: зРЖиІ£е§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛжШѓеР¶жНХжНЙзїУжЮДеМЦжДПдєЙпЉМйЬАи¶Бз†Фз©ґеЃГдїђе¶ВдљХи°®з§Їж¶ВењµеЕ≥з≥їгАВжЬђжЦЗжЧ®еЬ®жОҐзіҐLLMдЄ≠еЫЫзІНиѓ≠дєЙеЕ≥з≥їпЉИеРМдєЙгАБеПНдєЙгАБдЄКдљНгАБдЄЛдљНпЉЙзЪДзЉЦз†БжЦєеЉПеТМдљНзљЃгАВ

Method: зїУеРИзЇњжАІжОҐжµЛеТМжЬЇеИґеПѓиІ£йЗКжАІжКАжЬѓпЉМеМЕжЛђз®АзЦПиЗ™зЉЦз†БеЩ®еТМжњАжіїдњЃи°•пЉМз†Фз©ґдЄЙдЄ™дЄНеРМиІДж®°зЪДж®°еЮЛпЉИPythia-70MгАБGPT-2гАБLlama 3.1 8BпЉЙдЄ≠иѓ≠дєЙеЕ≥з≥їзЪДзЉЦз†БгАВ

Result: еПСзО∞е±ВзЇІеЕ≥з≥їе≠ШеЬ®жЦєеРСдЄНеѓєзІ∞жАІпЉЪдЄКдљНеЕ≥з≥їзЉЦз†БеЖЧдљЩдЄФжКµжКЧжКСеИґпЉМдЄЛдљНеЕ≥з≥їдЊЭиµЦзіІеЗСзЙєеЊБжЫіжШУиҐЂз†іеЭПпЉЫеЕ≥з≥їдњ°еПЈеЬ®дЄ≠йЧіе±ВжЬАеЉЇпЉМеЬ®жЃЛеЈЃеРО/MLPиЈѓеЊДдЄ≠жѓФж≥®жДПеКЫдЄ≠жЫіеЉЇпЉЫеПНдєЙеЕ≥з≥їжЬАеЃєжШУпЉМеРМдєЙеЕ≥з≥їжЬАйЪЊпЉЫеЫ†жЮЬжАІдЊЭиµЦж®°еЮЛиГљеКЫпЉМLlama 3.1дЄ≠SAEеЉХеѓЉзЪДдњЃи°•иГљеПѓйЭ†жФєеПШдњ°еПЈпЉМе∞Пж®°еЮЛдЄ≠еИЩиЊГеЉ±жИЦдЄНз®≥еЃЪгАВ

Conclusion: з†Фз©ґйШРжШОдЇЖLLMдЄ≠иѓ≠дєЙеЕ≥з≥їеПѓйЭ†и°®з§ЇзЪДдљНзљЃеТМжЦєеЉПпЉМжПРдЊЫдЇЖдЄАдЄ™еПѓе§НзО∞зЪДж°ЖжЮґпЉМе∞Жз®АзЦПзЙєеЊБдЄОжОҐжµЛзЇІеЫ†жЮЬиѓБжНЃиБФз≥їиµЈжЭ•пЉМдЄЇзРЖиІ£LLMе¶ВдљХзЉЦз†БзїУжЮДеМЦиѓ≠дєЙеЕ≥з≥їжПРдЊЫдЇЖжЦ∞иІБиІ£гАВ

Abstract: Understanding whether large language models (LLMs) capture structured meaning requires examining how they represent concept relationships. In this work, we study three models of increasing scale: Pythia-70M, GPT-2, and Llama 3.1 8B, focusing on four semantic relations: synonymy, antonymy, hypernymy, and hyponymy. We combine linear probing with mechanistic interpretability techniques, including sparse autoencoders (SAE) and activation patching, to identify where these relations are encoded and how specific features contribute to their representation. Our results reveal a directional asymmetry in hierarchical relations: hypernymy is encoded redundantly and resists suppression, while hyponymy relies on compact features that are more easily disrupted by ablation. More broadly, relation signals are diffuse but exhibit stable profiles: they peak in the mid-layers and are stronger in post-residual/MLP pathways than in attention. Difficulty is consistent across models (antonymy easiest, synonymy hardest). Probe-level causality is capacity-dependent: on Llama 3.1, SAE-guided patching reliably shifts these signals, whereas on smaller models the shifts are weak or unstable. Our results clarify where and how reliably semantic relations are represented inside LLMs, and provide a reproducible framework for relating sparse features to probe-level causal evidence.

</details>


### [189] [Adaptive Guidance for Retrieval-Augmented Masked Diffusion Models](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17677)
*Jaemin Kim,Jong Chul Ye*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: ARAMжПРеЗЇдЇЖдЄАзІНзФ®дЇОжО©з†БжЙ©жХ£ж®°еЮЛзЪДиЗ™йАВеЇФж£АзіҐеҐЮеЉЇж°ЖжЮґпЉМйАЪињЗеК®жАБи∞ГжХіжМЗеѓЉе∞ЇеЇ¶жЭ•зЉУиІ£ж£АзіҐдЄКдЄЛжЦЗеЩ™е£∞дЄОж®°еЮЛеПВжХ∞зЯ•иѓЖеЖ≤з™БзЪДйЧЃйҐШ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ж£АзіҐеҐЮеЉЇзФЯжИРеЬ®ж£АзіҐдЄКдЄЛжЦЗе≠ШеЬ®еЩ™е£∞гАБдЄНеПѓйЭ†жИЦдЄОж®°еЮЛеПВжХ∞зЯ•иѓЖдЄНдЄАиЗіжЧґпЉМдЉЪдЇІзФЯж£АзіҐ-еЕИй™МеЖ≤з™БпЉМйЩНдљОзФЯжИРиі®йЗПгАВињЩдЄ™йЧЃйҐШеЬ®иЗ™еЫЮељТиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдЄ≠еЈ≤жЬЙз†Фз©ґпЉМдљЖеЬ®еЯЇдЇОжЙ©жХ£зЪДиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдЄ≠е∞ЪжЬ™еЕЕеИЖжОҐзіҐпЉМеЫ†дЄЇињ≠дї£еОїеЩ™ињЗз®ЛдЄЇжХіеРИж£АзіҐдЄКдЄЛжЦЗеЄ¶жЭ•дЇЖзЛђзЙєжМСжИШ

Method: жПРеЗЇиЗ™йАВеЇФж£АзіҐеҐЮеЉЇжО©з†БжЙ©жХ£пЉИARAMпЉЙпЉМињЩжШѓдЄАзІНжЧ†йЬАиЃ≠зїГзЪДиЗ™йАВеЇФжМЗеѓЉж°ЖжЮґпЉМзФ®дЇОжО©з†БжЙ©жХ£ж®°еЮЛеЬ®RAGиЃЊзљЃдЄ≠гАВARAMж†єжНЃж£АзіҐдЄКдЄЛжЦЗеЉХиµЈзЪДеИЖеЄГеБПзІїзЪДдњ°еЩ™жѓФпЉМеЬ®еОїеЩ™ињЗз®ЛдЄ≠еК®жАБж†°еЗЖжМЗеѓЉе∞ЇеЇ¶

Result: еЬ®е§ЪдЄ™зЯ•иѓЖеѓЖйЫЖеЮЛйЧЃз≠ФеЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХдЄКзЪДеєњж≥ЫеЃЮй™Ми°®жШОпЉМARAMеЬ®жХідљУйЧЃз≠ФжАІиГљдЄКдЉШдЇОзЂЮдЇЙжАІRAGеЯЇзЇњжЦєж≥Х

Conclusion: ARAMйАЪињЗеК®жАБи∞ГжХіжМЗеѓЉе∞ЇеЇ¶пЉМеЬ®ж£АзіҐдЄКдЄЛжЦЗжПРдЊЫеПѓйЭ†зЇ†ж≠£иѓБжНЃжЧґеК†еЉЇжМЗеѓЉпЉМеЬ®дЄКдЄЛжЦЗдњ°еПЈеШИжЭВжИЦдЄНжФѓжМБжЧґжКСеИґжМЗеѓЉпЉМжЬЙжХИиІ£еЖ≥дЇЖжЙ©жХ£ж®°еЮЛдЄ≠ж£АзіҐ-еЕИй™МеЖ≤з™БйЧЃйҐШ

Abstract: Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) improves factual grounding by incorporating external knowledge into language model generation. However, when retrieved context is noisy, unreliable, or inconsistent with the model's parametric knowledge, it introduces retrieval-prior conflicts that can degrade generation quality. While this problem has been studied in autoregressive language models, it remains largely unexplored in diffusion-based language models, where the iterative denoising process introduces unique challenges for integrating retrieved context. In this work, we propose Adaptive Retrieval-Augmented Masked Diffusion (ARAM), a training-free adaptive guidance framework for Masked Diffusion Models (MDMs) in RAG settings. ARAM dynamically calibrates the guidance scale during denoising according to the Signal-to-Noise Ratio (SNR) of the distributional shift induced by retrieved context. Intuitively, the model strengthens guidance when the retrieved context provides reliable corrective evidence and suppresses it when the contextual signal is noisy or non-supportive. Extensive experiments on multiple knowledge-intensive QA benchmarks show that ARAM improves overall QA performance over competitive RAG baselines.

</details>


### [190] [Harm or Humor: A Multimodal, Multilingual Benchmark for Overt and Covert Harmful Humor](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17759)
*Ahmed Sharshar,Hosam Elgendy,Saad El Dine Ahmed,Yasser Rohaim,Yuxia Wang*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇдЄАдЄ™е§Ъж®°жАБгАБе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзЪДеєљйїШж£АжµЛеЯЇеЗЖпЉМеМЕеРЂжЦЗжЬђгАБеЫЊеГПеТМиІЖйҐСпЉМеМЇеИЖеЃЙеЕ®дЄОжЬЙеЃ≥еєљйїШпЉМеєґиѓДдЉ∞зО∞жЬЙж®°еЮЛзЪДжАІиГљеЈЃиЈЭгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ељУеЙНйЭЩжАБеЯЇеЗЖжЧ†ж≥ХжНХжНЙдЊЭиµЦжЦЗеМЦзїЖеЊЃеЈЃеИЂеТМйЪРеРЂзЇњзіҐзЪДйїСиЙ≤еєљйїШпЉМе≠ШеЬ®еЃЙеЕ®жМСжИШпЉМйЬАи¶БжЫіеЕ®йЭҐзЪДиѓДдЉ∞ж°ЖжЮґгАВ

Method: жЮДеїЇеМЕеРЂ3,000жЦЗжЬђгАБ6,000еЫЊеГПпЉИиЛ±йШњеПМиѓ≠пЉЙеТМ1,200иІЖйҐСпЉИиЛ±йШњеПКйАЪзФ®пЉЙзЪДжЙЛеЈ•ж†Зж≥®жХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉМе∞ЖеєљйїШеИЖдЄЇеЃЙеЕ®гАБжШЊеЉПжЬЙеЃ≥еТМйЪРеЉПжЬЙеЃ≥дЄЙз±їгАВ

Result: йЧ≠жЇРж®°еЮЛжШЊиСЧдЉШдЇОеЉАжЇРж®°еЮЛпЉМиЛ±йШњиѓ≠и®АйЧіе≠ШеЬ®жШОжШЊжАІиГљеЈЃиЈЭпЉМеЗЄжШЊйЬАи¶БеЯЇдЇОжЦЗеМЦзЪДжО®зРЖжДЯзЯ•еЃЙеЕ®еѓєйљРгАВ

Conclusion: йЬАи¶БеЉАеПСиГље§ЯзРЖиІ£жЦЗеМЦиГМжЩѓеТМйЪРеРЂжО®зРЖзЪДеєљйїШеЃЙеЕ®ж£АжµЛз≥їзїЯпЉМдї•еЇФеѓєе§Ъж®°жАБгАБе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзОѓеҐГдЄ≠зЪДеЃЙеЕ®жМСжИШгАВ

Abstract: Dark humor often relies on subtle cultural nuances and implicit cues that require contextual reasoning to interpret, posing safety challenges that current static benchmarks fail to capture. To address this, we introduce a novel multimodal, multilingual benchmark for detecting and understanding harmful and offensive humor. Our manually curated dataset comprises 3,000 texts and 6,000 images in English and Arabic, alongside 1,200 videos that span English, Arabic, and language-independent (universal) contexts. Unlike standard toxicity datasets, we enforce a strict annotation guideline: distinguishing \emph{Safe} jokes from \emph{Harmful} ones, with the latter further classified into \emph{Explicit} (overt) and \emph{Implicit} (Covert) categories to probe deep reasoning. We systematically evaluate state-of-the-art (SOTA) open and closed-source models across all modalities. Our findings reveal that closed-source models significantly outperform open-source ones, with a notable difference in performance between the English and Arabic languages in both, underscoring the critical need for culturally grounded, reasoning-aware safety alignment. \textcolor{red}{Warning: this paper contains example data that may be offensive, harmful, or biased.}

</details>


### [191] [CoVerRL: Breaking the Consensus Trap in Label-Free Reasoning via Generator-Verifier Co-Evolution](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17775)
*Teng Pan,Yuchen Yan,Zixuan Wang,Ruiqing Zhang,Gaiyang Han,Wanqi Zhang,Weiming Lu,Jun Xiao,Yongliang Shen*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: CoVerRLж°ЖжЮґйАЪињЗиЃ©еНХдЄ™ж®°еЮЛеЬ®зФЯжИРеЩ®еТМй™МиѓБеЩ®иІТиЙ≤йЧідЇ§жЫњпЉМзЫЄдЇТеЉХеѓЉжПРеНЗпЉМиІ£еЖ≥дЇЖжЧ†ж†Зз≠ЊеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†дЄ≠"еЕ±иѓЖйЩЈйШ±"йЧЃйҐШпЉМеЬ®жХ∞е≠¶жО®зРЖеЯЇеЗЖдЄКжѓФеЯЇзЇњжПРеНЗ4.7-5.9%гАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: жЧ†ж†Зз≠ЊеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†йАЪеЄЄдљњзФ®е§ЪжХ∞жКХз•®з≠Фж°ИдљЬдЄЇдЉ™ж†Зз≠ЊпЉМдљЖе≠ШеЬ®"еЕ±иѓЖйЩЈйШ±"йЧЃйҐШпЉЪйЪПзЭАиЃ≠зїГжЬАе§ІеМЦиЗ™дЄАиЗіжАІпЉМиЊУеЗЇе§Ъж†ЈжАІеі©жЇГпЉМж®°еЮЛдЉЪиЗ™дњ°еЬ∞еЉЇеМЦз≥їзїЯжАІйФЩиѓѓгАВ

Method: жПРеЗЇCoVerRLж°ЖжЮґпЉМиЃ©еНХдЄ™ж®°еЮЛеЬ®зФЯжИРеЩ®еТМй™МиѓБеЩ®иІТиЙ≤йЧідЇ§жЫњгАВе§ЪжХ∞жКХз•®дЄЇй™МиѓБеЩ®иЃ≠зїГжПРдЊЫжЬЙеЩ™е£∞дљЖжЬЙдњ°жБѓзЪДзЫСзЭ£пЉМиАМжФєињЫзЪДй™МиѓБеЩ®йАРж≠•дїОдЉ™ж†Зз≠ЊдЄ≠ињЗжї§иЗ™дЄАиЗійФЩиѓѓпЉМељҐжИРиЙѓжАІеЊ™зОѓгАВ

Result: еЬ®QwenеТМLlamaж®°еЮЛз≥їеИЧдЄКзЪДеЃЮй™МжШЊз§ЇпЉМCoVerRLеЬ®жХ∞е≠¶жО®зРЖеЯЇеЗЖдЄКжѓФжЧ†ж†Зз≠ЊеЯЇзЇњжПРеНЗ4.7-5.9%гАВиЗ™й™МиѓБеЗЖз°ЃзОЗдїОзЇ¶55%жПРеНЗеИ∞85%дї•дЄКпЉМиѓБеЃЮдЄ§зІНиГљеКЫзЬЯж≠£еЕ±еРМињЫеМЦгАВ

Conclusion: CoVerRLйАЪињЗзФЯжИРеЩ®еТМй™МиѓБеЩ®зЪДеЕ±еРМињЫеМЦпЉМжЬЙжХИйБњеЕНдЇЖжЧ†ж†Зз≠ЊеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†дЄ≠зЪДеЕ±иѓЖйЩЈйШ±пЉМзїіжМБдЇЖйЂШе•ЦеК±еЗЖз°ЃзОЗпЉМдЄЇе§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛзЪДжЧ†зЫСзЭ£жО®зРЖиГљеКЫжПРеНЗжПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЙжХИж°ЖжЮґгАВ

Abstract: Label-free reinforcement learning enables large language models to improve reasoning capabilities without ground-truth supervision, typically by treating majority-voted answers as pseudo-labels. However, we identify a critical failure mode: as training maximizes self-consistency, output diversity collapses, causing the model to confidently reinforce systematic errors that evade detection. We term this the consensus trap. To escape it, we propose CoVerRL, a framework where a single model alternates between generator and verifier roles, with each capability bootstrapping the other. Majority voting provides noisy but informative supervision for training the verifier, while the improving verifier progressively filters self-consistent errors from pseudo-labels. This co-evolution creates a virtuous cycle that maintains high reward accuracy throughout training. Experiments across Qwen and Llama model families demonstrate that CoVerRL outperforms label-free baselines by 4.7-5.9\% on mathematical reasoning benchmarks. Moreover, self-verification accuracy improves from around 55\% to over 85\%, confirming that both capabilities genuinely co-evolve.

</details>


### [192] [Process Supervision for Chain-of-Thought Reasoning via Monte Carlo Net Information Gain](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17815)
*Corentin Royer,Debarun Bhattacharjya,Gaetano Rossiello,Andrea Giovannini,Mennatallah El-Assady*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇеЯЇдЇОдњ°жБѓиЃЇиЗ™еК®зФЯжИРжО®зРЖж≠•й™§ж†Зз≠ЊзЪДжЦєж≥ХпЉМйЩНдљОиЃ°зЃЧе§НжЭВеЇ¶иЗ≥O(N)пЉМжПРеНЗLLMжО®зРЖзЫСзЭ£зЪДеПѓжЙ©е±ХжАІ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е§Ъж≠•жО®зРЖжПРеНЗLLMиГљеКЫдљЖеҐЮеК†йФЩиѓѓдЉ†жТ≠й£ОйЩ©пЉМзО∞жЬЙињЗз®Ле•ЦеК±ж®°еЮЛдЊЭиµЦжШВиіµзЪДдЇЇеЈ•ж†Зж≥®жИЦиЃ°зЃЧеѓЖйЫЖзЪДиЗ™еК®ж†Зж≥®пЉМйЬАи¶БжЫійЂШжХИзЪДиЗ™еК®ж†Зж≥®жЦєж≥Х

Method: еИ©зФ®дњ°жБѓиЃЇиЗ™еК®зФЯжИРж≠•й™§зЇІж†Зз≠ЊпЉМдЉ∞иЃ°жѓПдЄ™жО®зРЖж≠•й™§еѓєж≠£з°Ѓз≠Фж°ИдЉЉзДґзЪДељ±еУНпЉМжПРдЊЫж≠•й™§иі®йЗПдњ°еПЈпЉМе∞ЖиЃ°зЃЧе§НжЭВеЇ¶дїОO(N log N)йЩНдљОеИ∞O(N)

Result: еЬ®жХ∞е≠¶гАБPythonзЉЦз®ЛгАБSQLеТМзІСе≠¶йЧЃз≠Фз≠Йе§Ъж†ЈеМЦжО®зРЖеЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХдЄ≠пЉМиѓ•жЦєж≥ХеЬ®жЬАдљ≥KдЄ™иѓДдЉ∞иЃЊзљЃдЄ≠еЃЮзО∞дЇЖжЬЙжХИзЪДжАЭзїійУЊйАЙжЛ©

Conclusion: иѓ•жЦєж≥ХеЃЮзО∞дЇЖLLMжО®зРЖзЪДеПѓжЙ©е±ХйЂШжХИзЫСзЭ£пЉМзЙєеИЂйАВзФ®дЇОйФЩиѓѓдЉ†жТ≠еЕ≥йФЃзЪДдїїеК°

Abstract: Multi-step reasoning improves the capabilities of large language models (LLMs) but increases the risk of errors propagating through intermediate steps. Process reward models (PRMs) mitigate this by scoring each step individually, enabling fine-grained supervision and improved reliability. Existing methods for training PRMs rely on costly human annotations or computationally intensive automatic labeling. We propose a novel approach to automatically generate step-level labels using Information Theory. Our method estimates how each reasoning step affects the likelihood of the correct answer, providing a signal of step quality. Importantly, it reduces computational complexity to $\mathcal{O}(N)$, improving over the previous $\mathcal{O}(N \log N)$ methods. We demonstrate that these labels enable effective chain-of-thought selection in best-of-$K$ evaluation settings across diverse reasoning benchmarks, including mathematics, Python programming, SQL, and scientific question answering. This work enables scalable and efficient supervision of LLM reasoning, particularly for tasks where error propagation is critical.

</details>


### [193] [Text-to-Stage: Spatial Layouts from Long-form Narratives](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17832)
*Jefferson Hernandez,Swarnadeep Saha,Chenxi Whitehouse,Sanjeel Parekh,Calvin Murdock,Yuliang Li,W. Owen Brimijoin,Vamsi Krishna Ithapu,Ishwarya Ananthabhotla*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: иЃЇжЦЗз†Фз©ґиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдїОжЧ†зїУжЮДеМЦжЦЗжЬђдЄ≠ињЫи°Мз©ЇйЧіжО®зРЖзЪДиГљеКЫпЉМзЙєеИЂжШѓеПЩдЇЛеИ∞еЙІжЬђеЄГе±АзЪДдїїеК°пЉМжПРеЗЇдЇЖдЄАзІНзїУеРИжЛТзїЭзЫСзЭ£еЊЃи∞ГеТМеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†зЪДиЃ≠зїГжЦєж≥ХпЉМеЬ®еП§еЕЄиЛ±иѓ≠жЦЗе≠¶иѓ≠жЦЩдЄКеПЦеЊЧдЇЖдЉШдЇОеЯЇзЇњж®°еЮЛзЪДжХИжЮЬгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: з†Фз©ґиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдїОзЉЇдєПжШОз°Ѓз©ЇйЧігАБдљНзљЃжИЦеЕ≥з≥їзЇњзіҐзЪДжЦЗжЬђдЄ≠ињЫи°Мз©ЇйЧіжО®зРЖзЪДиГљеКЫпЉМж®°дїњдЇЇз±їиГљеКЫеєґиЗ™еК®еМЦеѓєдЄЛжЄЄе™ТдљУеЇФзФ®жЬЙзЫКзЪДињЗз®ЛгАВ

Method: еЉХеЕ•жИПеЙІе≠¶еРѓеПСзЪДз°ЃеЃЪжАІиѓДдЉ∞е•ЧдїґпЉМжПРеЗЇзїУеРИжЛТзїЭзЫСзЭ£еЊЃи∞ГпЉИдљњзФ®Best-of-NйЗЗж†ЈпЉЙеТМйАЪињЗGRPOињЫи°МеПѓй™МиѓБе•ЦеК±зЪДеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†зЪДиЃ≠зїГжО®зРЖжЦєж≥ХгАВ

Result: еЬ®еП§еЕЄиЛ±иѓ≠жЦЗе≠¶жЦЗжЬђиѓ≠жЦЩдЄКзЪДеЃЮй™Ми°®жШОпЉМиѓ•жЦєж≥ХеЬ®иІТиЙ≤ељТе±ЮгАБз©ЇйЧіеРИзРЖжАІеТМзІїеК®зїПжµОжАІз≠Йе§ЪдЄ™жМЗж†ЗдЄКдЉШдЇОеОЯеІЛж®°еЮЛпЉМеєґдЄФдЄОLLMдљЬдЄЇиѓДеИ§иАЕеТМдЄїиІВдЇЇз±їеБПе•љдњЭжМБдЄАиЗігАВ

Conclusion: иѓ•жЦєж≥ХжИРеКЯжПРеНЗдЇЖиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛдїОжЧ†зїУжЮДеМЦжЦЗжЬђдЄ≠ињЫи°Мз©ЇйЧіжО®зРЖзЪДиГљеКЫпЉМдЄЇеПЩдЇЛеИ∞еЙІжЬђеЄГе±АдїїеК°жПРдЊЫдЇЖжЬЙжХИзЪДиІ£еЖ≥жЦєж°ИпЉМеєґеЬ®е§ЪдЄ™иѓДдЉ∞зїіеЇ¶дЄКи°®зО∞еЗЇиЙ≤гАВ

Abstract: In this work, we probe the ability of a language model to demonstrate spatial reasoning from unstructured text, mimicking human capabilities and automating a process that benefits many downstream media applications. Concretely, we study the narrative-to-play task: inferring stage-play layouts (scenes, speaker positions, movements, and room types) from text that lacks explicit spatial, positional, or relational cues. We then introduce a dramaturgy-inspired deterministic evaluation suite and, finally, a training and inference recipe that combines rejection SFT using Best-of-N sampling with RL from verifiable rewards via GRPO. Experiments on a text-only corpus of classical English literature demonstrate improvements over vanilla models across multiple metrics (character attribution, spatial plausibility, and movement economy), as well as alignment with an LLM-as-a-judge and subjective human preferences.

</details>


### [194] [Event-Centric Human Value Understanding in News-Domain Texts: An Actor-Conditioned, Multi-Granularity Benchmark](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17838)
*Yao Wang,Xin Liu,Zhuochen Liu,Jiankang Chen,Adam Jatowt,Kyoungsook Kim,Noriko Kando,Haitao Yu*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: NEVUжШѓдЄАдЄ™жЦ∞йЧїдЇЛдїґдЄ≠ењГеМЦзЪДдЇЇз±їдїЈеАЉзРЖиІ£еЯЇеЗЖпЉМдЄУж≥®дЇОдЇЛеЃЮжЦ∞йЧїдЄ≠зЪДжЉФеСШжЭ°дїґеМЦгАБдЇЛдїґдЄ≠ењГеМЦеТМжЦєеРСжДЯзЯ•зЪДдїЈеАЉиѓЖеИЂ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: зО∞жЬЙзЪДдЇЇз±їдїЈеАЉжХ∞жНЃйЫЖеЬ®дЇЛеЃЮжЦ∞йЧїдїЈеАЉзРЖиІ£жЦєйЭҐе≠ШеЬ®дЄНиґ≥пЉЪиЃЄе§ЪжХ∞жНЃйЫЖжШѓжЉФеСШжЧ†еЕ≥зЪДпЉМдЊЭиµЦе≠§зЂЛиѓЭиѓ≠жИЦеРИжИРеЬЇжЩѓпЉМзЉЇдєПжШОз°ЃзЪДдЇЛдїґзїУжЮДжИЦдїЈеАЉжЦєеРС

Method: дїО2,865зѓЗиЛ±жЦЗжЦ∞йЧїжЦЗзЂ†жЮДеїЇNEVUеЯЇеЗЖпЉМйЗЗзФ®LLMиЊЕеК©зЪДж†Зж≥®жµБз®ЛпЉМеМЕеРЂйШґжЃµжАІй™МиѓБеТМйТИеѓєжАІдЇЇеЈ•еЃ°ж†ЄпЉМеЬ®еЫЫдЄ™иѓ≠дєЙеНХеЕГзЇІеИЂпЉИе≠РдЇЛдїґгАБи°МдЄЇе§НеРИдЇЛдїґгАБжХЕдЇЛе§НеРИдЇЛдїґгАБжЦЗзЂ†пЉЙињЫи°Мж†Зж≥®

Result: NEVUеМЕеРЂ45,793дЄ™еНХеЕГ-жЉФеСШеѓєеТМ168,061дЄ™жЬЙеРСдїЈеАЉеЃЮдЊЛпЉМдљњзФ®еМЕеРЂ54дЄ™зїЖз≤ТеЇ¶дїЈеАЉеТМ20дЄ™з≤Чз≤ТеЇ¶з±їеИЂзЪДеИЖе±ВдїЈеАЉз©ЇйЧіпЉМиљїйЗПзЇІйАВеЇФпЉИLoRAпЉЙиГљжМБзї≠жФєињЫеЉАжЇРж®°еЮЛ

Conclusion: NEVUдЄНдїЕжШѓдЄАдЄ™иѓДдЉ∞еЯЇеЗЖпЉМињШжФѓжМБиґЕиґКжПРз§ЇиѓДдЉ∞зЪДзЫСзЭ£йАВеЇФпЉМдЄЇдЇЛеЃЮжЦ∞йЧїдЄ≠зЪДдЇЇз±їдїЈеАЉзРЖиІ£жПРдЊЫдЇЖжЫіеЕ®йЭҐгАБзїУжЮДеМЦзЪДиѓДдЉ∞ж°ЖжЮґ

Abstract: Existing human value datasets do not directly support value understanding in factual news: many are actor-agnostic, rely on isolated utterances or synthetic scenarios, and lack explicit event structure or value direction. We present \textbf{NEVU} (\textbf{N}ews \textbf{E}vent-centric \textbf{V}alue \textbf{U}nderstanding), a benchmark for \emph{actor-conditioned}, \emph{event-centric}, and \emph{direction-aware} human value recognition in factual news. NEVU evaluates whether models can identify value cues, attribute them to the correct actor, and determine value direction from grounded evidence. Built from 2{,}865 English news articles, NEVU organizes annotations at four semantic unit levels (\textbf{Subevent}, \textbf{behavior-based composite event}, \textbf{story-based composite event}, and \textbf{Article}) and labels \mbox{(unit, actor)} pairs for fine-grained evaluation across local and composite contexts. The annotations are produced through an LLM-assisted pipeline with staged verification and targeted human auditing. Using a hierarchical value space with \textbf{54} fine-grained values and \textbf{20} coarse-grained categories, NEVU covers 45{,}793 unit--actor pairs and 168{,}061 directed value instances. We provide unified baselines for proprietary and open-source LLMs, and find that lightweight adaptation (LoRA) consistently improves open-source models, showing that although NEVU is designed primarily as a benchmark, it also supports supervised adaptation beyond prompting-only evaluation. Data availability is described in Appendix~\ref{app:data_code_availability}.

</details>


### [195] [How do LLMs Compute Verbal Confidence](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17839)
*Dharshan Kumaran,Arthur Conmy,Federico Barbero,Simon Osindero,Viorica Patraucean,Petar Velickovic*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: LLMsзЪДи®Аиѓ≠зљЃдњ°еЇ¶дЄНжШѓеН≥жЧґиЃ°зЃЧпЉМиАМжШѓз≠Фж°ИзФЯжИРжЧґиЗ™еК®иѓДдЉ∞еєґзЉУе≠ШпЉМйЪПеРОж£АзіҐиЊУеЗЇпЉМдї£и°®жѓФиѓНеЕГеѓєжХ∞ж¶ВзОЗжЫідЄ∞еѓМзЪДз≠Фж°Ииі®йЗПиѓДдЉ∞


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: и®Аиѓ≠зљЃдњ°еЇ¶иҐЂеєњж≥ЫзФ®дЇОдїОйїСзЫТLLMsдЄ≠жПРеПЦдЄНз°ЃеЃЪжАІдЉ∞иЃ°пЉМдљЖLLMsеЖЕйГ®е¶ВдљХзФЯжИРињЩдЇЫеИЖжХ∞дїНдЄНжЄЕж•ЪгАВз†Фз©ґжЧ®еЬ®жОҐз©ґдЄ§дЄ™йЧЃйҐШпЉЪзљЃдњ°еЇ¶дљХжЧґиЃ°зЃЧпЉИеН≥жЧґиѓЈж±ВжЧґињШжШѓз≠Фж°ИзФЯжИРжЧґиЗ™еК®иЃ°зЃЧеєґзЉУе≠ШпЉЙпЉМдї•еПКи®Аиѓ≠зљЃдњ°еЇ¶дї£и°®дїАдєИпЉИиѓНеЕГеѓєжХ∞ж¶ВзОЗињШжШѓжЫідЄ∞еѓМзЪДз≠Фж°Ииі®йЗПиѓДдЉ∞пЉЙ

Method: иБЪзД¶Gemma 3 27BеТМQwen 2.5 7Bж®°еЮЛпЉМдљњзФ®жњАжіїеЉХеѓЉгАБи°•дЄБгАБеЩ™е£∞еТМдЇ§жНҐеЃЮй™МпЉМж≥®жДПеКЫйШїжЦ≠жКАжЬѓпЉМдї•еПКзЇњжАІжОҐжµЛеТМжЦєеЈЃеИЖиІ£з≠ЙжЦєж≥ХпЉМеИЖжЮРзљЃдњ°еЇ¶и°®з§ЇзЪДеЗЇзО∞дљНзљЃеТМдњ°жБѓжµБ

Result: еПСзО∞зљЃдњ°еЇ¶и°®з§ЇеЬ®з≠Фж°ИзЫЄйВїдљНзљЃеЗЇзО∞пЉМжЧ©дЇОи®Аиѓ≠еМЦдљНзљЃпЉЫзљЃдњ°еЇ¶дїОз≠Фж°ИиѓНеЕГжФґйЫЖпЉМзЉУе≠ШеЬ®зђђдЄАдЄ™з≠Фж°ИеРОдљНзљЃпЉМзДґеРОж£АзіҐиЊУеЗЇпЉЫзЉУе≠ШзЪДи°®з§ЇиІ£йЗКдЇЖи®Аиѓ≠зљЃдњ°еЇ¶дЄ≠иґЕеЗЇиѓНеЕГеѓєжХ∞ж¶ВзОЗзЪДжШЊиСЧжЦєеЈЃпЉМи°®жШОжШѓжЫідЄ∞еѓМзЪДз≠Фж°Ииі®йЗПиѓДдЉ∞иАМйЭЮзЃАеНХзЪДжµБзХЕеЇ¶иѓїеПЦ

Conclusion: и®Аиѓ≠зљЃдњ°еЇ¶еПНжШ†дЇЖиЗ™еК®гАБе§НжЭВзЪДиЗ™жИСиѓДдЉ∞пЉМиАМйЭЮдЇЛеРОйЗНеїЇпЉМињЩеѓєзРЖиІ£LLMsзЪДеЕГиЃ§зЯ•еТМжФєињЫж†°еЗЖеЕЈжЬЙйЗНи¶БжДПдєЙ

Abstract: Verbal confidence -- prompting LLMs to state their confidence as a number or category -- is widely used to extract uncertainty estimates from black-box models. However, how LLMs internally generate such scores remains unknown. We address two questions: first, when confidence is computed - just-in-time when requested, or automatically during answer generation and cached for later retrieval; and second, what verbal confidence represents - token log-probabilities, or a richer evaluation of answer quality? Focusing on Gemma 3 27B and Qwen 2.5 7B, we provide convergent evidence for cached retrieval. Activation steering, patching, noising, and swap experiments reveal that confidence representations emerge at answer-adjacent positions before appearing at the verbalization site. Attention blocking pinpoints the information flow: confidence is gathered from answer tokens, cached at the first post-answer position, then retrieved for output. Critically, linear probing and variance partitioning reveal that these cached representations explain substantial variance in verbal confidence beyond token log-probabilities, suggesting a richer answer-quality evaluation rather than a simple fluency readout. These findings demonstrate that verbal confidence reflects automatic, sophisticated self-evaluation -- not post-hoc reconstruction -- with implications for understanding metacognition in LLMs and improving calibration.

</details>


### [196] [Mitigating LLM Hallucinations through Domain-Grounded Tiered Retrieval](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17872)
*Md. Asraful Haque,Aasar Mehdi,Maaz Mahboob,Tamkeen Fatima*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇеЯЇдЇОйҐЖеЯЯзЯ•иѓЖзЪДе±ВзЇІж£АзіҐдЄОй™МиѓБжЮґжЮДпЉМйАЪињЗеЫЫйШґжЃµиЗ™и∞ГиКВзЃ°йБУз≥їзїЯжЛ¶жИ™LLMеєїиІЙпЉМеЬ®е§ЪдЄ™еЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХдЄ≠жШЊиСЧдЉШдЇОйЫґж†ЈжЬђеЯЇзЇњ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛиЩљзДґжµБзХЕдљЖеЃєжШУдЇІзФЯ"еєїиІЙ"пЉИдЇЛеЃЮйФЩиѓѓжИЦжЬ™зїПй™МиѓБзЪДеЖЕеЃєпЉЙпЉМињЩеЬ®еПѓйЭ†жАІиЗ≥еЕ≥йЗНи¶БзЪДйЂШй£ОйЩ©йҐЖеЯЯдЄ≠е∞§дЄЇеЕ≥йФЃпЉМйЬАи¶Бз≥їзїЯжАІеЬ∞иІ£еЖ≥ињЩдЄАйЧЃйҐШ

Method: йЗЗзФ®еЫЫйШґжЃµиЗ™и∞ГиКВзЃ°йБУпЉЪ1) еЖЕеЬ®й™МиѓБдЄОжПРеЙНйААеЗЇйАїиЊСдЉШеМЦиЃ°зЃЧпЉЫ2) иЗ™йАВеЇФжРЬзіҐиЈѓзФ±еИ©зФ®йҐЖеЯЯж£АжµЛеЩ®еЃЪдљНзЙєеЃЪдЄїйҐШж°£ж°ИпЉЫ3) зЇ†ж≠£жАІжЦЗж°£еИЖзЇІињЗжї§жЧ†еЕ≥дЄКдЄЛжЦЗпЉЫ4) е§ЦйГ®еЖНзФЯдЄОеОЯе≠Ре£∞жШОзЇІй™МиѓБ

Result: еЬ®дЇФдЄ™еЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХзЪД650дЄ™жЯ•иѓҐдЄ≠пЉМзЃ°йБУеЬ®жЙАжЬЙзОѓеҐГдЄ≠жМБзї≠дЉШдЇОйЫґж†ЈжЬђеЯЇзЇњпЉМTimeQA v2иГЬзОЗиЊЊеИ∞83.7%пЉМMMLU Global FactsиЊЊеИ∞78.0пЉМеЯЇз°АжАІеИЖжХ∞з®≥еЃЪеЬ®78.8%иЗ≥86.4%дєЛйЧі

Conclusion: иѓ•жЮґжЮДдЄЇйФЩиѓѓдњ°жБѓжПРдЊЫдЇЖеЉЇе§ІзЪДжХЕйЪЬдњЭжК§пЉМдљЖиѓЖеИЂеЗЇ"иЩЪеБЗеЙНжПРињЗеЇ¶е£∞зІ∞"зЪДжМБзї≠е§±иі•ж®°еЉПпЉМжЬ™жЭ•еЈ•дљЬеЇФдЉШеЕИиАГиЩСйҐДж£АзіҐ"еПѓеЫЮз≠ФжАІ"иКВзВєдї•ињЫдЄАж≠•зЉ©е∞ПеѓєиѓЭAIзЪДеПѓйЭ†жАІеЈЃиЈЭ

Abstract: Large Language Models (LLMs) have achieved unprecedented fluency but remain susceptible to "hallucinations" - the generation of factually incorrect or ungrounded content. This limitation is particularly critical in high-stakes domains where reliability is paramount. We propose a domain-grounded tiered retrieval and verification architecture designed to systematically intercept factual inaccuracies by shifting LLMs from stochastic pattern-matchers to verified truth-seekers. The proposed framework utilizes a four-phase, self-regulating pipeline implemented via LangGraph: (I) Intrinsic Verification with Early-Exit logic to optimize compute, (II) Adaptive Search Routing utilizing a Domain Detector to target subject-specific archives, (III) Corrective Document Grading (CRAG) to filter irrelevant context, and (IV) Extrinsic Regeneration followed by atomic claim-level verification. The system was evaluated across 650 queries from five diverse benchmarks: TimeQA v2, FreshQA v2, HaluEval General, MMLU Global Facts, and TruthfulQA. Empirical results demonstrate that the pipeline consistently outperforms zero-shot baselines across all environments. Win rates peaked at 83.7% in TimeQA v2 and 78.0% in MMLU Global Facts, confirming high efficacy in domains requiring granular temporal and numerical precision. Groundedness scores remained robustly stable between 78.8% and 86.4% across factual-answer rows. While the architecture provides a robust fail-safe for misinformation, a persistent failure mode of "False-Premise Overclaiming" was identified. These findings provide a detailed empirical characterization of multi-stage RAG behavior and suggest that future work should prioritize pre-retrieval "answerability" nodes to further bridge the reliability gap in conversational AI.

</details>


### [197] [DebugLM: Learning Traceable Training Data Provenance for LLMs](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17884)
*Wenjie Jacky Mo,Qin Liu,Xiaofei Wen,Wenxuan Zhou,Zhe Zhao,Muhao Chen*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: DebugLMдЄЇLLMжПРдЊЫеЖЕзљЃжХ∞жНЃжЇѓжЇРиГљеКЫпЉМдљњж®°еЮЛиГљињљиЄ™и°МдЄЇеИ∞зЙєеЃЪиЃ≠зїГжХ∞жНЃжЇРпЉМжФѓжМБйТИеѓєжАІжµЛиѓХжЧґдњЃе§Н


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ељУеЙНLLMиЃ≠зїГдљњзФ®е§ЪйШґжЃµеЉВжЮДжХ∞жНЃзЃ°йБУпЉМдљЖеЉАеПСиАЕзЉЇдєПз≥їзїЯжЦєж≥ХжЭ•еЃЪдљНеѓЉиЗізЙєеЃЪи°МдЄЇзЪДиЃ≠зїГжХ∞жНЃпЉМињЩдљњеЊЧи∞ГиѓХеП™иГљиҐЂеК®дњЃи°•пЉМдЄФж®°еЮЛеЬ®еИЖеЄГеБПзІїжИЦжЫіжЦ∞еРОеЃєжШУйЗНзО∞е§±иі•

Method: жПРеЗЇDebugLMж°ЖжЮґпЉМиЃ©ж®°еЮЛе≠¶дє†е∞ЖеУНеЇФдЄОеФѓдЄАжЇѓжЇРж†Зз≠ЊеЕ≥иБФпЉМињЩдЇЫж†Зз≠ЊжМЗз§ЇиіЯиі£зЪДжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉЫеЯЇдЇОж≠§иГљеКЫжФѓжМБйТИеѓєжАІжµЛиѓХжЧґдњЃе§НпЉМеЕБиЃЄеЉАеПСиАЕйАЙжЛ©жАІиІ¶еПСеѓєжМЗеЃЪжХ∞жНЃжЇРзЪДйТИеѓєжАІжЛТзїЭпЉМжЧ†йЬАйЗНжЦ∞иЃ≠зїГжИЦдњЃжФєж®°еЮЛеПВжХ∞

Result: еЃЮй™Ми°®жШОDebugLMеЬ®е§ЪйШґжЃµиЃ≠зїГзЃ°йБУдЄ≠жПРдЊЫеЗЖз°ЃзЪДи°МдЄЇињљиЄ™пЉМеЃЮзО∞жЬЙжХИзЪДжµЛиѓХжЧґдњЃе§НпЉМеРМжЧґдњЭжМБж®°еЮЛзЪДйАЪзФ®жХИзФ®

Conclusion: DebugLMйАЪињЗдЄЇLLMжПРдЊЫеЖЕзљЃжХ∞жНЃжЇѓжЇРиГљеКЫпЉМиІ£еЖ≥дЇЖиЃ≠зїГжХ∞жНЃеПѓиІВжµЛжАІйЧЃйҐШпЉМдљњеЉАеПСиАЕиГљз≤Њз°ЃиѓЖеИЂдЄНиЙѓи°МдЄЇжЭ•жЇРеєґињЫи°МйТИеѓєжАІдњЃе§НпЉМжПРйЂШдЇЖLLMзЪДи∞ГиѓХеТМеПѓйЭ†жАІ

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) are trained through multi-stage pipelines over heterogeneous data sources, yet developers lack a principled way to pinpoint the specific data responsible for an observed behavior. This lack of observability reduces debugging to reactive patching and makes failures prone to recur under distribution shift or subsequent model updates. To address this limitation, we propose DebugLM, a framework that equips LLMs with built-in data provenance, enabling them to explicitly trace the origins of their behaviors to specific training data sources. Specifically, the model learns to associate its responses with unique provenance tags that indicate the responsible dataset, empowering developers to precisely identify where undesirable behaviors are learned. Building on this capability, DebugLM further supports targeted test-time remediation, enabling developers to selectively trigger targeted refusal for specified data sources without retraining or modifying model parameters. Experiments demonstrate that DebugLM provides accurate behavior tracing in multi-stage training pipelines and effective test-time remediation while preserving the general utility of the model.

</details>


### [198] [Pretrained Multilingual Transformers Reveal Quantitative Distance Between Human Languages](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17912)
*Yue Zhao,Jiatao Gu,Paloma JeretiƒН,Weijie Su*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жЬђжЦЗжПРеЗЇдЇЖдЄАзІНеИ©зФ®йҐДиЃ≠зїГе§Ъиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛзЪДж≥®жДПеКЫжЬЇеИґжЭ•йЗПеМЦиѓ≠и®АиЈЭз¶їзЪДжЦ∞жЦєж≥ХвАФвАФж≥®жДПеКЫдЉ†иЊУиЈЭз¶їпЉИATDпЉЙпЉМиѓ•жЦєж≥ХиГље§ЯжБҐе§НеЈ≤зЯ•зЪДиѓ≠и®АеИЖзїДеєґжПРеНЗдљОиµДжЇРжЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСжАІиГљгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: иЩљзДґиѓ≠и®Ае≠¶йХњжЬЯдї•жЭ•жПРдЊЫдЇЖдЄ∞еѓМзЪДиЈ®иѓ≠и®АеПШеЉВеЃЪжАІжППињ∞пЉМдљЖзЉЇдєПзїЯдЄАдЄФеПѓжЙ©е±ХзЪДйЗПеМЦжЦєж≥ХжЭ•жµЛйЗПиѓ≠и®АиЈЭз¶їгАВињЩзІНиЈЭз¶їжµЛйЗПеѓєдЇОиѓ≠и®Ае≠¶гАБдЇЇз±їе≠¶еТМдЇЇз±їињЫеМЦеОЖеП≤з†Фз©ґиЗ≥еЕ≥йЗНи¶БгАВ

Method: еИ©зФ®йҐДиЃ≠зїГе§Ъиѓ≠и®Аиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛзЪДж≥®жДПеКЫжЬЇеИґдљЬдЄЇиѓ≠и®АжµЛйЗПзЪДз≥їзїЯеЈ•еЕЈгАВе∞Жж≥®жДПеКЫзЯ©йШµиІЖдЄЇж¶ВзОЗеИЖеЄГпЉМйАЪињЗжЬАдЉШдЉ†иЊУжµЛйЗПеЕґеЗ†дљХеЈЃеЉВпЉМдїОиАМйЗПеМЦзњїиѓСињЗз®ЛдЄ≠зЪДиѓ≠и®Аи°®з§ЇиЈЭз¶їпЉМзІ∞дЄЇж≥®жДПеКЫдЉ†иЊУиЈЭз¶їпЉИATDпЉЙгАВ

Result: ATDеЇФзФ®дЇОе§ІйЗПе§Ъж†ЈеМЦиѓ≠и®АжЧґпЉМиГље§ЯйЂШдњЭзЬЯеЬ∞жБҐе§НеЈ≤зЯ•зЪДиѓ≠и®АеИЖзїДпЉМеєґжП≠з§ЇдЄОеЬ∞зРЖеТМжО•иІ¶иѓ±еѓЉеЕ≥з≥їдЄАиЗізЪДж®°еЉПгАВж≠§е§ЦпЉМе∞ЖATDдљЬдЄЇж≠£еИЩеМЦеЩ®еПѓдї•жФєеЦДдљОиµДжЇРжЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСзЪДињБзІїжАІиГљгАВ

Conclusion: иѓ•жЦєж≥ХдЄЇдљњзФ®дЇЇеЈ•з•ЮзїПзљСзїЬжµЛиѓХиѓ≠и®АеБЗиЃЊеїЇзЂЛдЇЖеОЯеИЩжАІеЯЇз°АпЉМе∞Же§Ъиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛиљђеМЦдЄЇеЉЇе§ІзЪДеЃЪйЗПиѓ≠и®АеПСзО∞еЈ•еЕЈпЉМдњГињЫжЫіеЕђеє≥зЪДе§Ъиѓ≠и®АдЇЇеЈ•жЩЇиГљеПСе±ХгАВ

Abstract: Understanding the distance between human languages is central to linguistics, anthropology, and tracing human evolutionary history. Yet, while linguistics has long provided rich qualitative accounts of cross-linguistic variation, a unified and scalable quantitative approach to measuring language distance remains lacking. In this paper, we introduce a method that leverages pretrained multilingual language models as systematic instruments for linguistic measurement. Specifically, we show that the spontaneously emerged attention mechanisms of these models provide a robust, tokenization-agnostic measure of cross-linguistic distance, termed Attention Transport Distance (ATD). By treating attention matrices as probability distributions and measuring their geometric divergence via optimal transport, we quantify the representational distance between languages during translation. Applying ATD to a large and diverse set of languages, we demonstrate that the resulting distances recover established linguistic groupings with high fidelity and reveal patterns aligned with geographic and contact-induced relationships. Furthermore, incorporating ATD as a regularizer improves transfer performance in low-resource machine translation. Our results establish a principled foundation for testing linguistic hypotheses using artificial neural networks. This framework transforms multilingual models into powerful tools for quantitative linguistic discovery, facilitating more equitable multilingual AI.

</details>


### [199] [IndicSafe: A Benchmark for Evaluating Multilingual LLM Safety in South Asia](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17915)
*Priyaranjan Pattnayak,Sanchari Chowdhuri*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: й¶ЦдЄ™йТИеѓє12зІНеН∞еЇ¶иѓ≠и®АзЪДе§Іиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛеЃЙеЕ®жАІиѓДдЉ∞пЉМжП≠з§ЇиЈ®иѓ≠и®АеЃЙеЕ®дЄАиЗіжАІдїЕ12.8%пЉМеЃЙеЕ®зОЗжЦєеЈЃиґЕ17%пЉМи°®жШОе§Ъиѓ≠и®АLLMе≠ШеЬ®дЄ•йЗНзЪДеЃЙеЕ®ж≥ЫеМЦеЈЃиЈЭгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛеЬ®е§Ъиѓ≠и®АзОѓеҐГдЄ≠йГ®зљ≤жЧґпЉМеЕґеЃЙеЕ®жАІеЬ®жЦЗеМЦе§Ъж†ЈгАБиµДжЇРеМЃдєПзЪДиѓ≠и®АдЄ≠и°®зО∞дїНдЄНжЄЕж•ЪгАВеН∞еЇ¶иѓ≠и®АжЬЙиґЕињЗ12дЇњдљњзФ®иАЕпЉМдљЖеЬ®LLMиЃ≠зїГжХ∞жНЃдЄ≠дї£и°®жАІдЄНиґ≥пЉМйЬАи¶Бз≥їзїЯиѓДдЉ∞еЕґеЃЙеЕ®жАІгАВ

Method: дљњзФ®еМЕеРЂ6,000дЄ™жЦЗеМЦзЫЄеЕ≥жПРз§ЇзЪДжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉМжґµзЫЦзІНеІУгАБеЃЧжХЩгАБжАІеИЂгАБеБ•еЇЈеТМжФњж≤їз≠ЙдЄїйҐШпЉМиѓДдЉ∞10дЄ™дЄїжµБLLMеЬ®12зІНеН∞еЇ¶иѓ≠и®АдЄКзЪДи°®зО∞гАВйАЪињЗзњїиѓСеПШдљУгАБжПРз§ЇзЇІзЖµгАБз±їеИЂеБПзљЃеИЖжХ∞еТМе§Ъиѓ≠и®АдЄАиЗіжАІжМЗжХ∞йЗПеМЦеЃЙеЕ®е§±иі•гАВ

Result: еПСзО∞жШЊиСЧзЪДеЃЙеЕ®жЉВзІїпЉЪиЈ®иѓ≠и®АдЄАиЗіжАІдїЕ12.8%пЉМеЃЙеЕ®зОЗжЦєеЈЃиґЕињЗ17%гАВдЄАдЇЫж®°еЮЛеЬ®дљОиµДжЇРиДЪжЬђдЄ≠ињЗеЇ¶жЛТзїЭиЙѓжАІжПРз§ЇпЉМињЗеЇ¶ж†ЗиЃ∞жФњж≤їжХПжДЯиѓЭйҐШпЉМиАМеЕґдїЦж®°еЮЛеИЩжЬ™иГљж†ЗиЃ∞дЄНеЃЙеЕ®зФЯжИРгАВ

Conclusion: е§Ъиѓ≠и®АLLMе≠ШеЬ®еЕ≥йФЃзЪДеЃЙеЕ®ж≥ЫеМЦеЈЃиЈЭпЉМеЃЙеЕ®еѓєйљРеЬ®дЄНеРМиѓ≠и®АйЧіиљђзІїдЄНеЭЗгАВеПСеЄГIndicSafeеЯЇеЗЖзФ®дЇОеН∞еЇ¶йГ®зљ≤зЪДжЦЗеМЦзЯ•жГЕеЃЙеЕ®иѓДдЉ∞пЉМеА°еѓЉеЯЇдЇОеМЇеЯЯеН±еЃ≥зЪДиѓ≠и®АжДЯзЯ•еѓєйљРз≠ЦзХ•гАВ

Abstract: As large language models (LLMs) are deployed in multilingual settings, their safety behavior in culturally diverse, low-resource languages remains poorly understood. We present the first systematic evaluation of LLM safety across 12 Indic languages, spoken by over 1.2 billion people but underrepresented in LLM training data. Using a dataset of 6,000 culturally grounded prompts spanning caste, religion, gender, health, and politics, we assess 10 leading LLMs on translated variants of the prompt.
  Our analysis reveals significant safety drift: cross-language agreement is just 12.8\%, and \texttt{SAFE} rate variance exceeds 17\% across languages. Some models over-refuse benign prompts in low-resource scripts, overflag politically sensitive topics, while others fail to flag unsafe generations. We quantify these failures using prompt-level entropy, category bias scores, and multilingual consistency indices.
  Our findings highlight critical safety generalization gaps in multilingual LLMs and show that safety alignment does not transfer evenly across languages. We release \textsc{IndicSafe}, the first benchmark to enable culturally informed safety evaluation for Indic deployments, and advocate for language-aware alignment strategies grounded in regional harms.

</details>


### [200] [Efficient Training-Free Multi-Token Prediction via Embedding-Space Probing](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17942)
*Raghavv Goel,Mukul Gagrani,Mingu Lee,Chris Lott*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇдЄАзІНжЧ†йЬАиЃ≠зїГзЪДе§Ъдї§зЙМйҐДжµЛжЦєж≥ХпЉМйАЪињЗжО©з†Бдї§зЙМжОҐжµЛLLMеЃЮзО∞еєґи°МйҐДжµЛпЉМжПРеНЗзФЯжИРеРЮеРРйЗП


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е∞љзЃ°LLMдїЕиЃ≠зїГзФ®дЇОдЄЛдЄАдЄ™дї§зЙМйҐДжµЛпЉМдљЖеЕЈжЬЙжљЬеЬ®зЪДе§Ъдї§зЙМйҐДжµЛиГљеКЫгАВзО∞жЬЙжЦєж≥ХйЬАи¶БдњЃжФєж®°еЮЛжЭГйЗНжИЦдЊЭиµЦиЊЕеК©иНЙж°Иж®°еЮЛпЉМжИСдїђеЄМжЬЫеЉАеПСдЄАзІНжЧ†йЬАиЃ≠зїГгАБжЧ†йЬАиЊЕеК©ж®°еЮЛзЪДзЃАеНХжЦєж≥ХгАВ

Method: 1) дљњзФ®жО©з†Бдї§зЙМдїОLLMеµМеЕ•з©ЇйЧіињЫи°МжОҐжµЛпЉЫ2) жЮДеїЇжО®жµЛдї§зЙМж†СпЉМдїОжО©з†Бдї§зЙМlogitsдЄ≠йЗЗж†Јtop-KеАЩйАЙпЉЫ3) еЇФзФ®иљїйЗПзЇІеЙ™жЮЭз≠ЦзХ•дњЭзХЩйЂШж¶ВзОЗеїґзї≠пЉЫ4) еЬ®иІ£з†БжЧґеєґи°Мй™МиѓБеАЩйАЙйҐДжµЛ

Result: еЬ®LLaMA3дЄКжО•еПЧйХњеЇ¶жПРеНЗзЇ¶12%пЉМеЬ®Qwen3дЄКжПРеНЗ8-12%пЉМеРЮеРРйЗПеҐЮзЫКиЊЊ15-19%пЉМдЉШдЇОзО∞жЬЙжЧ†йЬАиЃ≠зїГеЯЇзЇњжЦєж≥Х

Conclusion: иѓ•жЦєж≥ХеЃЮзО∞дЇЖжЧ†жНЯзФЯжИРпЉМжШЊиСЧеЗПе∞Сж®°еЮЛи∞ГзФ®жђ°жХ∞пЉМжПРеНЗдї§зЙМеРЮеРРйЗПгАВзРЖиЃЇеИЖжЮРеТМеЃЮиѓБиѓБжНЃи°®жШОиІ£з†Бе±ВиЗ™зДґеѓєйљРжО©з†Бдї§зЙМи°®з§ЇдЄОдЄЛдЄАдЄ™дї§зЙМзКґжАБпЉМжЧ†йЬАйЗНжЦ∞иЃ≠зїГжИЦиЊЕеК©ж®°еЮЛеН≥еПѓеЃЮзО∞еЗЖз°ЃзЪДе§Ъж≠•йҐДжµЛгАВ

Abstract: Large language models (LLMs) exhibit latent multi-token prediction (MTP) capabilities despite being trained solely for next-token generation. We propose a simple, training-free MTP approach that probes an LLM using on-the-fly mask tokens drawn from its embedding space, enabling parallel prediction of future tokens without modifying model weights or relying on auxiliary draft models. Our method constructs a speculative token tree by sampling top-K candidates from mask-token logits and applies a lightweight pruning strategy to retain high-probability continuations. During decoding, candidate predictions are verified in parallel, resulting in lossless generation while substantially reducing the number of model calls and improving token throughput. Across benchmarks, our probing-based MTP consistently outperforms existing training-free baselines, increasing acceptance length by approximately 12\% on LLaMA3 and 8--12\% on Qwen3, and achieving throughput gains of up to 15--19\%. Finally, we provide theoretical insights and empirical evidence showing that decoder layers naturally align mask-token representations with next-token states, enabling accurate multi-step prediction without retraining or auxiliary models.

</details>


### [201] [ShapleyLaw: A Game-Theoretic Approach to Multilingual Scaling Laws](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17945)
*Xuyang Cao,Qianying Liu,Chuan Xiao,Yusuke Oda,Pontus Stenetorp,Daisuke Kawahara,Makoto Onizuka,Sadao Kurohashi,Shuyuan Zheng*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жПРеЗЇShapleyLawпЉМдЄАзІНеЯЇдЇОеРИдљЬеНЪеЉИиЃЇзЪДе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзЉ©жФЊеЃЪеЊЛпЉМзФ®дЇОйЗПеМЦиЈ®иѓ≠и®АињБзІїжХИеЇФеєґдЉШеМЦйҐДиЃ≠зїГиѓ≠и®АжЈЈеРИжѓФдЊЛ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: ељУеЙНе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзЉ©жФЊеЃЪеЊЛжЧ†ж≥Хи°°йЗПиЈ®иѓ≠и®АињБзІїжХИеЇФпЉМеѓЉиЗійҐДиЃ≠зїГиѓ≠и®АжЈЈеРИжѓФдЊЛйАЙжЛ©жђ°дЉШгАВйЬАи¶БдЄАзІНиГљеЗЖз°ЃйЗПеМЦеРДиѓ≠и®Аиі°зМЃзЪДжЦєж≥ХжЭ•дЉШеМЦе§Ъиѓ≠и®АйҐДиЃ≠зїГ

Method: е∞Же§Ъиѓ≠и®АйҐДиЃ≠зїГеїЇж®°дЄЇеРИдљЬеНЪеЉИпЉМжѓПдЄ™иѓ≠и®АдљЬдЄЇзО©еЃґеЕ±еРМиі°зМЃйҐДиЃ≠зїГпЉМиОЈеЊЧжµЛиѓХжНЯе§±еЗПе∞СдљЬдЄЇжФґзЫКгАВдљњзФ®еРИдљЬеНЪеЉИиЃЇзЪДShapleyеАЉйЗПеМЦжѓПдЄ™иѓ≠и®АзЪДиі°зМЃпЉМжПРеЗЇShapleyLawе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзЉ©жФЊеЃЪеЊЛ

Result: еЃЮй™Ми°®жШОShapleyLawеЬ®ж®°еЮЛжАІиГљйҐДжµЛеТМиѓ≠и®АжЈЈеРИжѓФдЊЛдЉШеМЦжЦєйЭҐдЉШдЇОеЯЇзЇњжЦєж≥Х

Conclusion: еЯЇдЇОеРИдљЬеНЪеЉИиЃЇзЪДShapleyLawиГљжЬЙжХИйЗПеМЦиЈ®иѓ≠и®АињБзІїжХИеЇФпЉМдЄЇе§Ъиѓ≠и®АйҐДиЃ≠зїГжПРдЊЫжЫідЉШзЪДиѓ≠и®АжЈЈеРИжѓФдЊЛдЉШеМЦжЦєж≥Х

Abstract: In multilingual pretraining, the test loss of a pretrained model is heavily influenced by the proportion of each language in the pretraining data, namely the \textit{language mixture ratios}. Multilingual scaling laws can predict the test loss under different language mixture ratios and can therefore be used to estimate the optimal ratios. However, the current approaches to multilingual scaling laws do not measure the \textit{cross-lingual transfer} effect, resulting in suboptimal mixture ratios. In this paper, we consider multilingual pretraining as a cooperative game in which each language acts as a player that jointly contributes to pretraining, gaining the resulting reduction in test loss as the payoff. Consequently, from the perspective of cooperative game theory, we quantify the cross-lingual transfer from each language by its contribution in the game, and propose a game-theoretic multilingual scaling law called \textit{ShapleyLaw}. Our experiments show that ShapleyLaw outperforms baseline methods in model performance prediction and language mixture optimization.

</details>


### [202] [Gender Disambiguation in Machine Translation: Diagnostic Evaluation in Decoder-Only Architectures](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17952)
*Chiara Manna,Hosein Mohebbi,Afra Alishahi,Fr√©d√©ric Blain,Eva Vanmassenhove*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: жЬђжЦЗжПРеЗЇ"еЕИй™МеБПиІБ"жЦ∞жМЗж†ЗиѓДдЉ∞жЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСдЄ≠зЪДжАІеИЂеБПиІБпЉМеПСзО∞дїЕиІ£з†БеЩ®ж®°еЮЛеЬ®жАІеИЂжМЗж†ЗдЄКдЄНдЉШдЇОзЉЦз†БеЩ®-иІ£з†БеЩ®жЮґжЮДпЉМдљЖеРОиЃ≠зїГиГљеЗПе∞СзФЈжАІеЕИй™МеБПиІБгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: е§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛеЬ®NLPдїїеК°дЄ≠и°®зО∞дЉШеЉВдљЖдїНе≠ШеЬ®з≥їзїЯжАІеБПиІБпЉМзЙєеИЂжШѓжЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСдЄ≠зЪДжАІеИЂеБПиІБгАВзО∞жЬЙеЯЇеЗЖжµЛиѓХжЧ†ж≥ХеЕ®йЭҐеПНжШ†зО∞дї£жЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСдЄ≠жАІеИЂеБПиІБзЪДе§НжЭВжАІпЉМйЬАи¶БжЫіз≤ЊзїЖзЪДиѓДдЉ∞ж°ЖжЮґгАВ

Method: жЙ©е±ХзО∞жЬЙеБПиІБиѓДдЉ∞ж°ЖжЮґпЉЪ(1) еЉХеЕ•"еЕИй™МеБПиІБ"жЦ∞жМЗж†ЗпЉМжНХжНЙж®°еЮЛзЪДйїШиЃ§жАІеИЂеБЗиЃЊпЉЫ(2) е∞Жиѓ•ж°ЖжЮґеЇФзФ®дЇОдїЕиІ£з†БеЩ®зЪДжЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСж®°еЮЛгАВ

Result: дїЕиІ£з†БеЩ®ж®°еЮЛеЬ®жАІеИЂзЙєеЃЪжМЗж†ЗдЄКйАЪеЄЄдЄНдЉШдЇОзЉЦз†БеЩ®-иІ£з†БеЩ®жЮґжЮДпЉЫдљЖеРОиЃ≠зїГпЉИе¶ВжМЗдї§еЊЃи∞ГпЉЙдЄНдїЕиГљжПРйЂШдЄКдЄЛжЦЗжДЯзЯ•иГљеКЫпЉМињШиГљеЗПе∞СзФЈжАІеЕИй™МеБПиІБгАВ

Conclusion: йЬАи¶БжЫіз≤ЊзїЖзЪДиѓДдЉ∞жЦєж≥ХжЭ•еЕ®йЭҐзРЖиІ£жЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСдЄ≠зЪДжАІеИЂеБПиІБпЉМеРОиЃ≠зїГжШѓеЗПе∞СжАІеИЂеБПиІБзЪДйЗНи¶БйАФеЊДпЉМдїЕиІ£з†БеЩ®жЮґжЮДеЬ®жАІеИЂеБПиІБжЦєйЭҐж≤°жЬЙжШОжШЊдЉШеКњгАВ

Abstract: While Large Language Models achieve state-of-the-art results across a wide range of NLP tasks, they remain prone to systematic biases. Among these, gender bias is particularly salient in MT, due to systematic differences across languages in whether and how gender is marked. As a result, translation often requires disambiguating implicit source signals into explicit gender-marked forms. In this context, standard benchmarks may capture broad disparities but fail to reflect the full complexity of gender bias in modern MT. In this paper, we extend recent frameworks on bias evaluation by: (i) introducing a novel measure coined "Prior Bias", capturing a model's default gender assumptions, and (ii) applying the framework to decoder-only MT models. Our results show that, despite their scale and state-of-the-art status, decoder-only models do not generally outperform encoder-decoder architectures on gender-specific metrics; however, post-training (e.g., instruction tuning) not only improves contextual awareness but also reduces the masculine Prior Bias.

</details>


### [203] [ConGA: Guidelines for Contextual Gender Annotation. A Framework for Annotating Gender in Machine Translation](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.17962)
*Argentina Anna Rescigno,Eva Vanmassenhove,Johanna Monti*

Main category: cs.CL

TL;DR: иѓ•иЃЇжЦЗжПРеЗЇдЇЖConGAж°ЖжЮґпЉМзФ®дЇОж†Зж≥®иЛ±иѓ≠еИ∞жДПе§ІеИ©иѓ≠зњїиѓСдЄ≠зЪДжАІеИЂдњ°жБѓпЉМдї•иІ£еЖ≥жЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСдЄ≠зЪДжАІеИЂеБПиІБйЧЃйҐШгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: жЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСеТМе§ІеЮЛиѓ≠и®Аж®°еЮЛеЬ®е§ДзРЖиЈ®иѓ≠и®АжАІеИЂдњ°жБѓжЧґйЭҐдЄіжМСжИШпЉМзЙєеИЂжШѓдїОжАІеИЂдЄ≠зЂЛиѓ≠и®АпЉИе¶ВиЛ±иѓ≠пЉЙзњїиѓСеИ∞ељҐжАБдЄКжЬЙжАІеИЂж†ЗиЃ∞зЪДиѓ≠и®АпЉИе¶ВжДПе§ІеИ©иѓ≠пЉЙгАВиЛ±иѓ≠зЉЇдєПиѓ≠ж≥ХжАІеИЂпЉМиАМжДПе§ІеИ©иѓ≠и¶Бж±Ве§ЪдЄ™иѓ≠ж≥ХиМГзХізЪДжАІеИЂдЄАиЗіпЉМињЩзІНдЄНеѓєзІ∞еѓЉиЗіMTз≥їзїЯйїШиЃ§дљњзФ®йШ≥жАІељҐеЉПпЉМеК†еЙІеБПиІБеєґйЩНдљОзњїиѓСеЗЖз°ЃжАІгАВ

Method: жПРеЗЇдЇЖдЄКдЄЛжЦЗжАІеИЂж†Зж≥®пЉИConGAпЉЙж°ЖжЮґпЉМињЩжШѓдЄАе•ЧеЯЇдЇОиѓ≠и®Ае≠¶зЪДиѓНзЇІжАІеИЂж†Зж≥®жМЗеНЧгАВиѓ•жЦєж°ИеМЇеИЖиЛ±иѓ≠дЄ≠зЪДиѓ≠дєЙжАІеИЂпЉИйШ≥жАІMгАБйШіжАІFгАБж®°з≥КAпЉЙеТМжДПе§ІеИ©иѓ≠дЄ≠зЪДиѓ≠ж≥ХжАІеИЂеЃЮзО∞пЉИйШ≥жАІMгАБйШіжАІFпЉЙпЉМеєґзїУеРИеЃЮдљУзЇІж†ЗиѓЖзђ¶ињЫи°МиЈ®еП•е≠РиЈЯиЄ™гАВе∞ЖConGAеЇФзФ®дЇОgENder-ITжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉМеИЫеїЇдЇЖиѓДдЉ∞зњїиѓСдЄ≠жАІеИЂеБПиІБзЪДйїДйЗСж†ЗеЗЖиµДжЇРгАВ

Result: зїУжЮЬжШЊз§Їз≥їзїЯжАІзЪДйШ≥жАІињЗеЇ¶дљњзФ®еТМдЄНдЄАиЗізЪДйШіжАІеЃЮзО∞пЉМз™БжШЊдЇЖељУеЙНMTз≥їзїЯзЪДжМБзї≠е±АйЩРжАІгАВйАЪињЗзїУеРИзїЖз≤ТеЇ¶иѓ≠и®Аж†Зж≥®еТМеЃЪйЗПиѓДдЉ∞пЉМжП≠з§ЇдЇЖжАІеИЂеБПиІБзЪДж®°еЉПгАВ

Conclusion: ињЩй°єеЈ•дљЬжПРдЊЫдЇЖжЦєж≥ХиЃЇеТМеЯЇеЗЖпЉМзФ®дЇОжЮДеїЇжЫіеЕЈжАІеИЂжДПиѓЖеТМе§Ъиѓ≠и®АзЪДNLPз≥їзїЯпЉМдЄЇеЗПе∞СжЬЇеЩ®зњїиѓСдЄ≠зЪДжАІеИЂеБПиІБжПРдЊЫдЇЖеЃЮзФ®еЈ•еЕЈеТМиѓДдЉ∞ж°ЖжЮґгАВ

Abstract: Handling gender across languages remains a persistent challenge for Machine Translation (MT) and Large Language Models (LLMs), especially when translating from gender-neutral languages into morphologically gendered ones, such as English to Italian. English largely omits grammatical gender, while Italian requires explicit agreement across multiple grammatical categories. This asymmetry often leads MT systems to default to masculine forms, reinforcing bias and reducing translation accuracy. To address this issue, we present the Contextual Gender Annotation (ConGA) framework, a linguistically grounded set of guidelines for word-level gender annotation. The scheme distinguishes between semantic gender in English through three tags, Masculine (M), Feminine (F), and Ambiguous (A), and grammatical gender realisation in Italian (Masculine (M), Feminine (F)), combined with entity-level identifiers for cross-sentence tracking. We apply ConGA to the gENder-IT dataset, creating a gold-standard resource for evaluating gender bias in translation. Our results reveal systematic masculine overuse and inconsistent feminine realisation, highlighting persistent limitations of current MT systems. By combining fine-grained linguistic annotation with quantitative evaluation, this work offers both a methodology and a benchmark for building more gender-aware and multilingual NLP systems.

</details>


<div id='cs.LG'></div>

# cs.LG [[Back]](#toc)

### [204] [A foundation model for electrodermal activity data](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16878)
*Leonardo Alchieri,Matteo Garzon,Lidia Alecci,Francesco Bombassei De Bona,Martin Gjoreski,Giovanni De Felice,Silvia Santini*

Main category: cs.LG

TL;DR: з†Фз©ґдЇЇеСШеИЫеїЇдЇЖEDAMAMEжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉИеМЕеРЂ24дЄ™еЕђеЉАжХ∞жНЃйЫЖзЪД25,000е§Ъе∞ПжЧґEDAдњ°еПЈпЉЙеєґиЃ≠зїГдЇЖй¶ЦдЄ™EDAдЄУзФ®еЯЇз°Аж®°еЮЛUMEпЉМиѓ•ж®°еЮЛеЬ®е§ЪжХ∞еЬЇжЩѓдЄ≠дЉШдЇОеЯЇзЇњдЄФиЃ°зЃЧиµДжЇРжґИиАЧеЗПе∞С20еАНгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: EDAпЉИзЪЃиВ§зФµжіїеК®пЉЙдљЬдЄЇеПНжШ†дЇ§жДЯз•ЮзїПз≥їзїЯжіїеК®зЪДйЗНи¶БзФЯзРЖдњ°еПЈпЉМеЬ®иЃ§зЯ•иіЯиНЈгАБеОЛеКЫеТМеПВдЄОеЇ¶иѓДдЉ∞дЄ≠еєњж≥ЫеЇФзФ®пЉМдљЖзЉЇдєПе§ІиІДж®°гАБеЉАжФЊеПѓиЃњйЧЃзЪДжХ∞жНЃйЫЖйШїзҐНдЇЖиѓ•йҐЖеЯЯеЯЇз°Аж®°еЮЛзЪДеПСе±ХгАВ

Method: 1) дїО24дЄ™еЕђеЉАжХ∞жНЃйЫЖдЄ≠жФґйЫЖеєґжХізРЖEDAMAMEжХ∞жНЃйЫЖпЉМеМЕеРЂ634еРНзФ®жИЈзЪД25,000е§Ъе∞ПжЧґEDAдњ°еПЈпЉЫ2) еЯЇдЇОиѓ•жХ∞жНЃйЫЖиЃ≠зїГUMEпЉМй¶ЦдЄ™дЄУйЧ®йТИеѓєEDAзЪДеЯЇз°Аж®°еЮЛгАВ

Result: UMEеЬ®10дЄ™еЬЇжЩѓдЄ≠зЪД8дЄ™и°®зО∞дЉШдЇОеЯЇзЇњж®°еЮЛпЉМдЄОйАЪзФ®жЧґйЧіеЇПеИЧеЯЇз°Аж®°еЮЛжАІиГљзЫЄељУпЉМеРМжЧґиЃ°зЃЧиµДжЇРжґИиАЧеЗПе∞С20еАНгАВз†Фз©ґињШжП≠з§ЇдЇЖEDAеїЇж®°зЪДеЖЕеЬ®жМСжИШгАВ

Conclusion: EDAMAMEжХ∞жНЃйЫЖеТМUMEж®°еЮЛдЄЇEDAз†Фз©ґжПРдЊЫдЇЖйЗНи¶БиµДжЇРпЉМдљЖEDAеїЇж®°дїНйЭҐдЄіжМСжИШпЉМйЬАи¶БињЫдЄАж≠•з†Фз©ґдї•еЕЕеИЖеПСжМ•еЕґжљЬеКЫгАВжЙАжЬЙжХ∞жНЃйЫЖгАБж®°еЮЛжЭГйЗНеТМдї£з†БеЭЗеЈ≤еЉАжЇРгАВ

Abstract: Foundation models have recently extended beyond natural language and vision to timeseries domains, including physiological signals. However, progress in electrodermal activity (EDA) modeling is hindered by the absence of large-scale, curated, and openly accessible datasets. EDA reflects sympathetic nervous system activity and is widely used to infer cognitive load, stress, and engagement. Yet very few wearable devices provide continuous, unobtrusive sensing, and the only large-scale archive to date is proprietary. To address this gap, we compile EDAMAME, a collection of EDA traces from 24 public datasets, comprising more than 25,000 hours from 634 users. Using this resource, we train UME, the first dedicated foundation model for EDA. In eight out of ten scenarios, UME outperforms baselines and matches generalist timeseries foundation models while using 20x fewer computational resources. Our findings, however, also highlight the intrinsic challenges of EDA modeling, motivating further research to unlock its full potential. All datasets, model weights, and code are released to support further research.

</details>


### [205] [Federated Multi Agent Deep Learning and Neural Networks for Advanced Distributed Sensing in Wireless Networks](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16881)
*Nadine Muller,Stefano DeRosa,Su Zhang,Chun Lee Huan*

Main category: cs.LG

TL;DR: ињЩзѓЗзїЉињ∞иЃЇжЦЗз≥їзїЯжҐ≥зРЖдЇЖ2021-2025еєійЧіе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУжЈ±еЇ¶е≠¶дє†еЬ®еИЖеЄГеЉПжДЯзЯ•еТМжЧ†зЇњйАЪдњ°йҐЖеЯЯзЪДжЬАжЦ∞ињЫе±ХпЉМжПРеЗЇдЇЖдїїеК°й©±еК®зЪДеИЖз±їдљУз≥їпЉМеєґе±ХжЬЫдЇЖйЭҐеРС6GзЪДжДЯзЯ•-йАЪдњ°-иЃ°зЃЧ-е≠¶дє†дЄАдљУеМЦз≥їзїЯз†Фз©ґжЦєеРСгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: йЪПзЭА5G-AdvancedеТМ6GжДњжЩѓзЪДеПСе±ХпЉМжЧ†зЇњз≥їзїЯдЄ≠жДЯзЯ•гАБйАЪдњ°еТМиЃ°зЃЧзЪДиА¶еРИжЧ•зЫКзіІеѓЖпЉМељҐжИРдЇЖеОїдЄ≠ењГеМЦгАБйГ®еИЖеПѓиІВжµЛгАБжЧґеПШдЄФиµДжЇРеПЧйЩРзЪДжОІеИґйЧЃйҐШгАВе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУжЈ±еЇ¶е≠¶дє†дЄЇж≠§з±їйЧЃйҐШжПРдЊЫдЇЖзїЯдЄАж°ЖжЮґпЉМйЬАи¶Бз≥їзїЯжҐ≥зРЖиѓ•йҐЖеЯЯзЪДжЬАжЦ∞ињЫе±ХгАВ

Method: йЗЗзФ®дїїеК°й©±еК®зЪДеИЖз±їжЦєж≥ХпЉМдїОеЫЫдЄ™зїіеЇ¶жЮДеїЇеИЖз±їдљУз≥їпЉЪ(1)е≠¶дє†ељҐеЉПеМЦпЉИй©ђе∞ФеПѓе§ЂеНЪеЉИгАБDec-POMDPsгАБCTDEпЉЙпЉМ(2)з•ЮзїПзљСзїЬжЮґжЮДпЉИеЯЇдЇОGNNзЪДжЧ†зЇњиµДжЇРзЃ°зРЖгАБеЯЇдЇОж≥®жДПеКЫзЪДз≠ЦзХ•гАБеИЖе±Ве≠¶дє†гАБз©ЇдЄ≠иБЪеРИпЉЙпЉМ(3)еЕИињЫжКАжЬѓпЉИиБФйВ¶еЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†гАБйАЪдњ°йЂШжХИзЪДиБФйВ¶жЈ±еЇ¶RLгАБжЧ†жЬНеК°еЩ®иЊєзЉШе≠¶дє†зЉЦжОТпЉЙпЉМ(4)еЇФзФ®йҐЖеЯЯпЉИMECеНЄиљљдЄОеИЗзЙЗгАБUAVеЉВжЮДзљСзїЬгАБдЉ†жДЯеЩ®зљСзїЬеЕ•дЊµж£АжµЛгАБISACй©±еК®зЪДжДЯзЯ•зІїеК®зљСзїЬпЉЙгАВ

Result: жПРдЊЫдЇЖзЃЧж≥ХгАБиЃ≠зїГжЛУжЙСеТМз≥їзїЯзЇІжЭГи°°пЉИеїґињЯгАБйҐСи∞±жХИзОЗгАБиГљиАЧгАБйЪРзІБгАБй≤Бж£ТжАІпЉЙзЪДжѓФиЊГи°®пЉМз≥їзїЯжАїзїУдЇЖе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУжЈ±еЇ¶е≠¶дє†еЬ®жЧ†зЇњйАЪдњ°йҐЖеЯЯзЪДжЬАжЦ∞з†Фз©ґжИРжЮЬгАВ

Conclusion: иЃЇжЦЗиѓЖеИЂдЇЖеПѓжЙ©е±ХжАІгАБйЭЮеє≥з®≥жАІгАБеЃЙеЕ®йШ≤жК§гАБйАЪдњ°еЉАйФАеТМеЃЮжЧґеЃЙеЕ®жАІз≠ЙеЉАжФЊжАІйЧЃйҐШпЉМеєґе±ХжЬЫдЇЖйЭҐеРС6GеОЯзФЯжДЯзЯ•-йАЪдњ°-иЃ°зЃЧ-е≠¶дє†з≥їзїЯзЪДз†Фз©ґжЦєеРСпЉМдЄЇжЬ™жЭ•з†Фз©ґжПРдЊЫдЇЖйЗНи¶БеПВиАГгАВ

Abstract: Multi-agent deep learning (MADL), including multi-agent deep reinforcement learning (MADRL), distributed/federated training, and graph-structured neural networks, is becoming a unifying framework for decision-making and inference in wireless systems where sensing, communication, and computing are tightly coupled. Recent 5G-Advanced and 6G visions strengthen this coupling through integrated sensing and communication, edge intelligence, open programmable RAN, and non-terrestrial/UAV networking, which create decentralized, partially observed, time-varying, and resource-constrained control problems. This survey synthesizes the state of the art, with emphasis on 2021-2025 research, on MADL for distributed sensing and wireless communications. We present a task-driven taxonomy across (i) learning formulations (Markov games, Dec-POMDPs, CTDE), (ii) neural architectures (GNN-based radio resource management, attention-based policies, hierarchical learning, and over-the-air aggregation), (iii) advanced techniques (federated reinforcement learning, communication-efficient federated deep RL, and serverless edge learning orchestration), and (iv) application domains (MEC offloading with slicing, UAV-enabled heterogeneous networks with power-domain NOMA, intrusion detection in sensor networks, and ISAC-driven perceptive mobile networks). We also provide comparative tables of algorithms, training topologies, and system-level trade-offs in latency, spectral efficiency, energy, privacy, and robustness. Finally, we identify open issues including scalability, non-stationarity, security against poisoning and backdoors, communication overhead, and real-time safety, and outline research directions toward 6G-native sense-communicate-compute-learn systems.

</details>


### [206] [Multi-Agent Reinforcement Learning for Dynamic Pricing: Balancing Profitability,Stability and Fairness](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16888)
*Krishna Kumar Neelakanta Pillai Santha Kumari Amma*

Main category: cs.LG

TL;DR: жЬђжЦЗз≥їзїЯиѓДдЉ∞дЇЖMAPPOеТМMADDPGдЄ§зІНе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†жЦєж≥ХеЬ®зЂЮдЇЙжАІйЫґеФЃеЄВеЬЇеК®жАБеЃЪдїЈдЄ≠зЪДеЇФзФ®пЉМеПСзО∞MAPPOеЬ®еИ©жґ¶еТМз®≥еЃЪжАІжЦєйЭҐи°®зО∞жЬАдљ≥пЉМиАМMADDPGеЬ®еИ©жґ¶еИЖйЕНеЕђеє≥жАІжЦєйЭҐжЬАдЉШгАВ


<details>
  <summary>Details</summary>
Motivation: зЂЮдЇЙжАІйЫґеФЃеЄВеЬЇзЪДеК®жАБеЃЪдїЈйЬАи¶БиГље§ЯйАВеЇФйЬАж±Вж≥ҐеК®еТМзЂЮдЇЙеѓєжЙЛи°МдЄЇзЪДз≠ЦзХ•гАВзО∞жЬЙзЛђзЂЛе≠¶дє†жЦєж≥ХеПѓиГљжЧ†ж≥ХеЕЕеИЖе§ДзРЖе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУзОѓеҐГдЄ≠зЪДе§НжЭВдЇ§дЇТпЉМеЫ†ж≠§йЬАи¶БиѓДдЉ∞е§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†жЦєж≥ХеЬ®еК®жАБеЃЪдїЈдЉШеМЦдЄ≠зЪДи°®зО∞гАВ

Method: дљњзФ®еЯЇдЇОзЬЯеЃЮйЫґеФЃжХ∞жНЃжЮДеїЇзЪДж®°жЛЯеЄВеЬЇзОѓеҐГпЉМз≥їзїЯиѓДдЉ∞MAPPOеТМMADDPGдЄ§зІНе§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†зЃЧж≥ХгАВдї•зЛђзЂЛDDPGпЉИIDDPGпЉЙдљЬдЄЇеЯЇзЇњжЦєж≥ХињЫи°МеѓєжѓФпЉМдїОеИ©жґ¶и°®зО∞гАБйЪПжЬЇзІНе≠Рз®≥еЃЪжАІгАБеЕђеє≥жАІеТМиЃ≠зїГжХИзОЗеЫЫдЄ™зїіеЇ¶ињЫи°МзїЉеРИиѓДдЉ∞гАВ

Result: MAPPOеЬ®еє≥еЭЗжФґзЫКжЦєйЭҐи°®зО∞жЬАдљ≥дЄФжЦєеЈЃжЬАдљОпЉМжПРдЊЫдЇЖз®≥еЃЪеПѓе§НзО∞зЪДзЂЮдЇЙеЃЪдїЈдЉШеМЦжЦєж≥ХпЉЫMADDPGиЩљзДґеИ©жґ¶зХ•дљОпЉМдљЖеЬ®жЩЇиГљдљУйЧіеИ©жґ¶еИЖйЕНеЕђеє≥жАІжЦєйЭҐи°®зО∞жЬАдЉШгАВдЄ§зІНMARLжЦєж≥ХйГљдЉШдЇОзЛђзЂЛе≠¶дє†еЯЇзЇњгАВ

Conclusion: е§ЪжЩЇиГљдљУеЉЇеМЦе≠¶дє†жЦєж≥ХпЉИзЙєеИЂжШѓMAPPOпЉЙдЄЇеК®жАБйЫґеФЃеЃЪдїЈжПРдЊЫдЇЖеПѓжЙ©е±ХдЄФз®≥еЃЪзЪДжЫњдї£жЦєж°ИпЉМдЉШдЇОдЉ†зїЯзЪДзЛђзЂЛе≠¶дє†жЦєж≥ХпЉМиГље§ЯеЬ®зЂЮдЇЙзОѓеҐГдЄ≠еЃЮзО∞жЫіе•љзЪДеИ©жґ¶и°®зО∞еТМз®≥еЃЪжАІгАВ

Abstract: Dynamic pricing in competitive retail markets requires strategies that adapt to fluctuating demand and competitor behavior. In this work, we present a systematic empirical evaluation of multi-agent reinforcement learning (MARL) approaches-specifically MAPPO and MADDPG-for dynamic price optimization under competition. Using a simulated marketplace environment derived from real-world retail data, we benchmark these algorithms against an Independent DDPG (IDDPG) baseline, a widely used independent learner in MARL literature. We evaluate profit performance, stability across random seeds, fairness, and training efficiency. Our results show that MAPPO consistently achieves the highest average returns with low variance, offering a stable and reproducible approach for competitive price optimization, while MADDPG achieves slightly lower profit but the fairest profit distribution among agents. These findings demonstrate that MARL methods-particularly MAPPO-provide a scalable and stable alternative to independent learning approaches for dynamic retail pricing.

</details>


### [207] [From Language to Action in Arabic: Reliable Structured Tool Calling via Data-Centric Fine-Tuning](https://arxiv.org/abs/2603.16901)
